revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「'''巨勢 黒麻呂'''（こせ の くろまろ）は、[[飛鳥時代]]の豪族。名は'''黒丸'''とも記される<ref>『公卿補任』天平9年…」
revision.text:
'''巨勢 黒麻呂'''（こせ の くろまろ）は、[[飛鳥時代]]の豪族。名は'''黒丸'''とも記される<ref>『公卿補任』天平9年巨勢朝臣堺麿の条</ref>。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[臣]]。[[左大臣]]・[[巨勢徳多]]の子で、[[巨勢邑治|邑治]]・[[巨勢小邑治|小邑治]]の父。[[冠位二十六階|冠位]]は[[小錦中]]・[[中納言]]だが、後述するように、官職については疑義がある。
== 考証 == その経歴や人生については、不明な点が多いが、『[[続日本紀]]』の[[巨勢邑治]]の薨伝<ref>『続日本紀』神亀元年6月6日条</ref>から、以下のことが分かる。 #彼の冠位である[[小錦中]]は、[[天智天皇]]3年（[[664年]]）2月制定の[[冠位二十六階]]の第11等であり、高位ではあった。しかし、[[天武天皇]]14年（[[685年]]正月には[[冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|冠位四十八階]]が制定されている。 #[[中納言]]は[[慶雲]]2年（[[705年]]）4月制定の[[令外官]]であり、天智・天武朝には存在していない。天武朝になって設置され、「納言」に、後世になって「中」の文字を挿入したものと推定される<ref>[[青木和夫]]「[[田中卓]]氏の「中納言（その一）」を読む」『続日本紀研究』一 - 八</ref>。納言の文字は天武天皇9年（[[680年]]）の[[舎人王]]や<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇下 9年7月25日条</ref>、持統天皇元年（[[687年]]）正月の[[阿倍御主人|布勢御主人]]にも見え<ref>『日本書紀』持統天皇元年正月1日条</ref>、天武朝では大臣が置かれなかったために、納言が最高の行政官であった。納言の官名中国由来のもので『[[書経]]』「舜典」に見えるもので、[[隋]]・[[唐]]の初期には[[門下省]]の長官（侍中）の称号であった。
以上のように、天武朝の高位・高職であり、天武天皇14年（[[685年]]）までになくなっている。
== 系譜 == *父：巨勢徳多 *母：不詳 *生母不明の子女 **男子：巨勢邑治 **男子：巨勢小邑治
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『日本書紀』(五)、[[岩波文庫]]、1995年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳(下）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年 * 『続日本紀』2 新日本古典文学大系13岩波書店、1990年 * [[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社学術文庫]]、1992年
{{DEFAULTSORT:こせ の くろまろ}} [[Category:巨勢氏|くろまろ]] [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物]] [[Category:生年不明]] [[Category:7世紀没]]

revision.comment: Created page with "[[ja:小錦中]] '''Shōkinchū''' (小錦中) was a [[冠位|court rank]] used in Japan from [[664]] to [[685]]. It was the 11th rank in a 26-rank system, positioned below {{ill|Shōkinjō|ja|小錦上}} and above {{ill|Shōkinge|ja|小錦下}}. == Overview == Shōkinchū was established on the 9th day of the 2nd month in the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s reign (664) as part of the {{ill|26-rank cap system|ja|冠位二十六階}}. It replaced the..."
revision.text:
[[ja:小錦中]] '''Shōkinchū''' (小錦中) was a [[冠位|court rank]] used in Japan from [[664]] to [[685]]. It was the 11th rank in a 26-rank system, positioned below {{ill|Shōkinjō|ja|小錦上}} and above {{ill|Shōkinge|ja|小錦下}}.
== Overview == Shōkinchū was established on the 9th day of the 2nd month in the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}'s reign (664) as part of the {{ill|26-rank cap system|ja|冠位二十六階}}. It replaced the two ranks of {{ill|Kobana Jō|ja|小花上}} and {{ill|Kobana Ge|ja|小花下}}, which were reorganized into the three ranks of Shōkinjō, Shōkinchū, and Shōkinge.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|3|2|9}}.</ref> Under the {{ill|Taika Reforms|ja|大化改新}} of 647, a rank named {{ill|Shōkin|ja|小錦}} already existed but was divided into Kobana Jō and Kobana Ge in 649. Shōkinge and related ranks were thus a revival of the earlier naming convention.
This rank was abolished on the 21st day of the 1st month in the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}'s reign (685), when the naming conventions for ranks were completely revised.
== Notable Individuals == The following individuals held the rank of Shōkinchū, as recorded in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}: * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenji|8|}}.</ref> * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Conducted rituals at the Hirose River in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Tasked with recording imperial genealogies and ancient affairs in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|10|3|17}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Surveyed the Kinai region for suitable capital sites in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|2|28}}.</ref>
Posthumous promotions to the rank of {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}} were conferred on the following individuals: * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|9|5|27}}.</ref> * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682).<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|11|7|9}}.</ref>
Later sources such as the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}} also mention: * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - A middle counselor (chūnagon).<ref>{{shokunihongi|神亀|1|6|6}}.</ref> * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Possibly held the rank, though an inscription on his tomb suggests {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}.<ref>[http://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/syuzou/meihin/kouko/item09.html Kyoto National Museum, "Gold-Copper Tomb Inscription of Ono no Azumabito"] (retrieved June 2009).</ref>
== List of Individuals == * {{ill|Kawachi Kujira|ja|河内鯨}} - Recognized in the 8th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (669) as an envoy to Tang China. * {{ill|Maishin no Oogai|ja|間人大蓋}} - Recognized in the 4th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (675). * {{ill|Ono no Azumabito|ja|小野毛人}} - Died in the 6th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (677), though his actual rank may have been {{ill|Daikinjō|ja|大錦上}}. * {{ill|Hoshikawa no Maro|ja|星川麻呂}} - Died in the 9th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (680), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Inabe no Shikō|ja|忌部子首}} - Recognized in the 10th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (681). * {{ill|Kashiwade no Maro|ja|膳摩漏}} - Died in the 11th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (682), posthumously promoted to {{ill|Daishi|ja|大紫}}. * {{ill|Ōtomo no Yasumaro|ja|大伴安麻呂}} - Recognized in the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu's reign (684). * {{ill|Kose no Kurohito|ja|巨勢黒麻呂}} - Middle counselor (chūnagon).
== References == {{reflist}}

[[zh:小錦中]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kose no Kurohito-qq]] [[ja:巨勢黒麻呂]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kose no Kurohito-qq]] [[ja:冠位・位階制度の変遷]]
[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Kose no Kurohito-qq]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:Shōkinchū]][[Category:Japanese court rank system]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Shōkinchū]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Shōkinchū forks]] [[Category:Kose no Kurohito]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:q--1]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] '''巨勢 黒麻呂'''（こせ の くろまろ）は、[[飛鳥時代]]の[[貴族]]。名は'''黒丸'''とも記される<ref>『公卿補任』天平9年巨勢朝臣堺麿の条</ref>。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[臣]]。[[左大臣]]・[[巨勢徳多]]の子で、[[巨勢邑治|邑治]]・[[巨勢小邑治|小邑治]]の父。[[冠位二十六階|冠位]]は[[小錦中]]・[[中納言]]だが、後述するように、官職については疑義がある。
== 考証 == その経歴や人生については、不明な点が多いが、『[[続日本紀]]』の[[巨勢邑治]]の薨伝<ref>『続日本紀』神亀元年6月6日条</ref>から、以下のことが分かる。 #彼の冠位である[[小錦中]]は、[[天智天皇]]3年（[[664年]]）2月制定の[[冠位二十六階]]の第11等であり、高位ではあった。しかし、[[天武天皇]]14年（[[685年]]）正月には[[冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|冠位四十八階]]が制定されている。 #[[中納言]]は[[慶雲]]2年（[[705年]]）4月制定の[[令外官]]であり、天智・天武朝には存在していない。天武朝になって設置され、「納言」に、後世になって「中」の文字を挿入したものと推定される<ref>[[青木和夫]]「[[田中卓]]氏の「中納言（その一）」を読む」『続日本紀研究』一 - 八</ref>。納言の文字は天武天皇9年（[[680年]]）の[[舎人王]]や<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇下 9年7月25日条</ref>、持統天皇元年（[[687年]]）正月の[[阿倍御主人|布勢御主人]]にも見え<ref>『日本書紀』持統天皇元年正月1日条</ref>、天武朝では大臣が置かれなかったために、納言が最高の行政官であった。納言の官名は中国由来のもので『[[書経]]』「舜典」に見えるもので、[[隋]]・[[唐]]の初期には[[門下省]]の長官（侍中）の称号であった。
以上のように、天武朝の高位・高官職の人物であり、天武天皇14年（[[685年]]）までに亡くなっている。
== 系譜 == *父：巨勢徳多 *母：不詳 *生母不明の子女 **男子：[[巨勢邑治]] **男子：[[巨勢小邑治]]
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

[[Category:q--1]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] '''巨勢 黒麻呂'''（こせ の くろまろ）は、[[飛鳥時代]]の[[貴族]]。名は'''黒丸'''とも記される<ref>『公卿補任』天平9年巨勢朝臣堺麿の条</ref>。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[臣]]。[[左大臣]]・[[巨勢徳多]]の子で、[[巨勢邑治|邑治]]・[[巨勢小邑治|小邑治]]の父。[[冠位二十六階|冠位]]は[[小錦中]]・[[中納言]]だが、後述するように、官職については疑義がある。
== 考証 == その経歴や人生については、不明な点が多いが、『[[続日本紀]]』の[[巨勢邑治]]の薨伝<ref>『続日本紀』神亀元年6月6日条</ref>から、以下のことが分かる。 #彼の冠位である[[小錦中]]は、[[天智天皇]]3年（[[664年]]）2月制定の[[冠位二十六階]]の第11等であり、高位ではあった。しかし、[[天武天皇]]14年（[[685年]]）正月には[[冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|冠位四十八階]]が制定されている。 #[[中納言]]は[[慶雲]]2年（[[705年]]）4月制定の[[令外官]]であり、天智・天武朝には存在していない。天武朝になって設置され、「納言」に、後世になって「中」の文字を挿入したものと推定される<ref>[[青木和夫]]「[[田中卓]]氏の「中納言（その一）」を読む」『続日本紀研究』一 - 八</ref>。納言の文字は天武天皇9年（[[680年]]）の[[舎人王]]や<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇下 9年7月25日条</ref>、持統天皇元年（[[687年]]）正月の[[阿倍御主人|布勢御主人]]にも見え<ref>『日本書紀』持統天皇元年正月1日条</ref>、天武朝では大臣が置かれなかったために、納言が最高の行政官であった。納言の官名は中国由来のもので『[[書経]]』「舜典」に見えるもので、[[隋]]・[[唐]]の初期には[[門下省]]の長官（侍中）の称号であった。
以上のように、天武朝の高位・高官職の人物であり、天武天皇14年（[[685年]]）までに亡くなっている。
== 系譜 == *父：巨勢徳多 *母：不詳 *生母不明の子女 **男子：[[巨勢邑治]] **男子：[[巨勢小邑治]]

== 考証 == その経歴や人生については、不明な点が多いが、『[[続日本紀]]』の[[巨勢邑治]]の薨伝<ref>『続日本紀』神亀元年6月6日条</ref>から、以下のことが分かる。 #彼の冠位である[[小錦中]]は、[[天智天皇]]3年（[[664]]）2月制定の[[冠位二十六階]]の第11等であり、高位ではあった。しかし、[[天武天皇]]14年（[[685]]）正月には[[冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|冠位四十八階]]が制定されている。 #[[中納言]]は[[慶雲]]2年（[[705]]）4月制定の[[令外官]]であり、天智・天武朝には存在していない。天武朝になって設置され、「納言」に、後世になって「中」の文字を挿入したものと推定される<ref>[[青木和夫]]「[[田中卓]]氏の「中納言（その一）」を読む」『続日本紀研究』一 - 八</ref>。納言の文字は天武天皇9年（[[680]]）の[[舎人王]]や<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇下 9年7月25日条</ref>、持統天皇元年（[[687]]）正月の[[阿倍御主人|布勢御主人]]にも見え<ref>『日本書紀』持統天皇元年正月1日条</ref>、天武朝では大臣が置かれなかったために、納言が最高の行政官であった。納言の官名は中国由来のもので『[[書経]]』「舜典」に見えるもので、[[隋]]・[[唐]]の初期には[[門下省]]の長官（侍中）の称号であった。
以上のように、天武朝の高位・高官職の人物であり、天武天皇14年（[[685]]）までに亡くなっている。
== 系譜 == *父：巨勢徳多 *母：不詳 *生母不明の子女 **男子：[[巨勢邑治]] **男子：[[巨勢小邑治]]
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『日本書紀』(五)、[[岩波文庫]]、1995年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳(下）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年 * 『続日本紀』2 新日本古典文学大系13[[岩波書店]]、1990年 * [[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社学術文庫]]、1992年

== 考証 == その経歴や人生については、不明な点が多いが、『[[続日本紀]]』の[[巨勢邑治]]の薨伝<ref>『続日本紀』神亀元年6月6日条</ref>から、以下のことが分かる。 #彼の冠位である[[小錦中]]は、[[天智天皇]]3年（[[664]]）2月制定の[[冠位二十六階]]の第11等であり、高位ではあった。しかし、[[天武天皇]]14年（[[685]]）正月には[[冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|冠位四十八階]]が制定されている。 #[[中納言]]は[[慶雲]]2年（[[705]]）4月制定の[[令外官]]であり、天智・天武朝には存在していない。天武朝になって設置され、「納言」に、後世になって「中」の文字を挿入したものと推定される<ref>[[青木和夫]]「[[田中卓]]氏の「中納言（その一）」を読む」『続日本紀研究』一 - 八</ref>。納言の文字は天武天皇9年（[[680]]）の[[舎人王]]や<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇下 9年7月25日条</ref>、持統天皇元年（[[687]]）正月の[[阿倍御主人|布勢御主人]]にも見え<ref>『日本書紀』持統天皇元年正月1日条</ref>、天武朝では大臣が置かれなかったために、納言が最高の行政官であった。納言の官名は中国由来のもので『[[書経]]』「舜典」に見えるもので、[[隋]]・[[唐]]の初期には[[門下省]]の長官（侍中）の称号であった。
以上のように、天武朝の高位・高官職の人物であり、天武天皇14年（[[685]]）までに亡くなっている。
== 系譜 == *父：巨勢徳多 *母：不詳 *生母不明の子女 **男子：[[巨勢邑治]] **男子：[[巨勢小邑治]]
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『日本書紀』(五)、[[岩波文庫]]、1995年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳(下）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年 * 『続日本紀』2 新日本古典文学大系13[[岩波書店]]、1990年 * [[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社学術文庫]]、1992年
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{people-stub}} {{citation needed|こせ の くろまろ}} [[Category:巨勢氏|くろまろ]] [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物]] [[Category:7世紀生]] [[Category:7世紀没]]

[[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:ose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] '''巨勢 黒麻呂'''（こせ の くろまろ）は、[[飛鳥時代]]の[[貴族]]。名は'''黒丸'''とも記される<ref>『公卿補任』天平9年巨勢朝臣堺麿の条</ref>。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[臣]]。[[左大臣]]・[[巨勢徳多]]の子で、[[巨勢邑治|邑治]]・[[巨勢小邑治|小邑治]]の父。[[冠位二十六階|冠位]]は[[小錦中]]・[[中納言]]だが、後述するように、官職については疑義がある。
== 考証 == その経歴や人生については、不明な点が多いが、『[[続日本紀]]』の[[巨勢邑治]]の薨伝<ref>『続日本紀』神亀元年6月6日条</ref>から、以下のことが分かる。 #彼の冠位である[[小錦中]]は、[[天智天皇]]3年（[[664]]）2月制定の[[冠位二十六階]]の第11等であり、高位ではあった。しかし、[[天武天皇]]14年（[[685]]）正月には[[冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|冠位四十八階]]が制定されている。 #[[中納言]]は[[慶雲]]2年（[[705]]）4月制定の[[令外官]]であり、天智・天武朝には存在していない。天武朝になって設置され、「納言」に、後世になって「中」の文字を挿入したものと推定される<ref>[[青木和夫]]「[[田中卓]]氏の「中納言（その一）」を読む」『続日本紀研究』一 - 八</ref>。納言の文字は天武天皇9年（[[680]]）の[[舎人王]]や<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇下 9年7月25日条</ref>、持統天皇元年（[[687]]）正月の[[阿倍御主人|布勢御主人]]にも見え<ref>『日本書紀』持統天皇元年正月1日条</ref>、天武朝では大臣が置かれなかったために、納言が最高の行政官であった。納言の官名は中国由来のもので『[[書経]]』「舜典」に見えるもので、[[隋]]・[[唐]]の初期には[[門下省]]の長官（侍中）の称号であった。
以上のように、天武朝の高位・高官職の人物であり、天武天皇14年（[[685]]）までに亡くなっている。
== 系譜 == *父：巨勢徳多 *母：不詳 *生母不明の子女 **男子：[[巨勢邑治]] **男子：[[巨勢小邑治]]
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]
[[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] '''巨勢 黒麻呂'''（こせ の くろまろ）は、[[飛鳥時代]]の[[貴族]]。名は'''黒丸'''とも記される<ref>『公卿補任』天平9年巨勢朝臣堺麿の条</ref>。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[臣]]。[[左大臣]]・[[巨勢徳多]]の子で、[[巨勢邑治|邑治]]・[[巨勢小邑治|小邑治]]の父。[[冠位二十六階|冠位]]は[[小錦中]]・[[中納言]]だが、後述するように、官職については疑義がある。
== 考証 == その経歴や人生については、不明な点が多いが、『[[続日本紀]]』の[[巨勢邑治]]の薨伝<ref>『続日本紀』神亀元年6月6日条</ref>から、以下のことが分かる。 #彼の冠位である[[小錦中]]は、[[天智天皇]]3年（[[664]]）2月制定の[[冠位二十六階]]の第qq等であり、高位ではあった。しかし、[[天武天皇]]14年（[[685]]）正月には[[冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|冠位四十八階]]が制定されている。 #[[中納言]]は[[慶雲]]2年（[[705]]）4月制定の[[令外官]]であり、天智・天武朝には存在していない。天武朝になって設置され、「納言」に、後世になって「中」の文字を挿入したものと推定される<ref>[[青木和夫]]「[[田中卓]]氏の「中納言（その一）」を読む」『続日本紀研究』一 - 八</ref>。納言の文字は天武天皇9年（[[680]]）の[[舎人王]]や<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇下 9年7月25日条</ref>、持統天皇元年（[[687]]）正月の[[阿倍御主人|布勢御主人]]にも見え<ref>『日本書紀』持統天皇元年正月1日条</ref>、天武朝では大臣が置かれなかったために、納言が最高の行政官であった。納言の官名は中国由来のもので『[[書経]]』「舜典」に見えるもので、[[隋]]・[[唐]]の初期には[[門下省]]の長官（侍中）の称号であった。
以上のように、天武朝の高位・高官職の人物であり、天武天皇14年（[[685]]）までに亡くなっている。
== 系譜 == *父：巨勢徳多 *母：不詳 *生母不明の子女 **男子：[[巨勢邑治]] **男子：[[巨勢小邑治]]

== 考証 == その経歴や人生については、不明な点が多いが、『[[続日本紀]]』の[[巨勢邑治]]の薨伝<ref>『続日本紀』神亀元年6月6日条</ref>から、以下のことが分かる。 #彼の冠位である[[小錦中]]は、[[天智天皇]]3年（[[664]]）2月制定の[[冠位二十六階]]の第qq等であり、高位ではあった。しかし、[[天武天皇]]14年（[[685]]）正月には[[冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|冠位四十八階]]が制定されている。 #[[中納言]]は[[慶雲]]2年（[[705]]）4月制定の[[令外官]]であり、天智・天武朝には存在していない。天武朝になって設置され、「納言」に、後世になって「中」の文字を挿入したものと推定される<ref>[[青木和夫]]「[[田中卓]]氏の「中納言（その一）」を読む」『続日本紀研究』一 - 八</ref>。納言の文字は天武天皇9年（[[680]]）の[[舎人王]]や<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇下 9年7月25日条</ref>、持統天皇元年（[[687]]）正月の[[阿倍御主人|布勢御主人]]にも見え<ref>『日本書紀』持統天皇元年正月1日条</ref>、天武朝では大臣が置かれなかったために、納言が最高の行政官であった。納言の官名は中国由来のもので『[[書経]]』「舜典」に見えるもので、[[隋]]・[[唐]]の初期には[[門下省]]の長官（侍中）の称号であった。
以上のように、天武朝の高位・高官職の人物であり、天武天皇14年（[[685]]）までに亡くなっている。
== 系譜 == *父：巨勢徳多 *母：不詳 *生母不明の子女 **男子：[[巨勢邑治]] **男子：[[巨勢小邑治]]
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

== 考証 == その経歴や人生については、不明な点が多いが、『[[続日本紀]]』の[[巨勢邑治]]の薨伝<ref>『続日本紀』神亀元年6月6日条</ref>から、以下のことが分かる。 #彼の冠位である[[小錦中]]は、[[天智天皇]]3年（[[664]]）2月制定の[[冠位二十六階]]の第qq等であり、高位ではあった。しかし、[[天武天皇]]14年（[[685]]）正月には[[冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|冠位四十八階]]が制定されている。 #[[中納言]]は[[慶雲]]2年（[[705]]）4月制定の[[令外官]]であり、天智・天武朝には存在していない。天武朝になって設置され、「納言」に、後世になって「中」の文字を挿入したものと推定される<ref>[[青木和夫]]「[[田中卓]]氏の「中納言（その一）」を読む」『続日本紀研究』一 - 八</ref>。納言の文字は天武天皇9年（[[680]]）の[[舎人王]]や<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇下 9年7月25日条</ref>、持統天皇元年（[[687]]）正月の[[阿倍御主人|布勢御主人]]にも見え<ref>『日本書紀』持統天皇元年正月1日条</ref>、天武朝では大臣が置かれなかったために、納言が最高の行政官であった。納言の官名は中国由来のもので『[[書経]]』「舜典」に見えるもので、[[隋]]・[[唐]]の初期には[[門下省]]の長官（侍中）の称号であった。
以上のように、天武朝の高位・高官職の人物であり、天武天皇14年（[[685]]）までに亡くなっている。

[[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]]{{citation needed|Kose no Kurohito}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kose no Kurohito}}[[Category:qq]] <!--Kose no Kurohito--> [[Category:bump]] [[Category:Shōkinchū]][[Category:Japanese court rank system]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Shōkinchū]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Shōkinchū forks]] [[Category:Kose no Kurohito]]
[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]
[[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:qq]] '''巨勢 黒麻呂'''（こせ の くろまろ）は、[[飛鳥時代]]の[[貴族]]。名は'''黒丸'''とも記される<ref>『公卿補任』天平9年巨勢朝臣堺麿の条</ref>。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[臣]]。[[左大臣]]・[[巨勢徳多]]の子で、[[巨勢邑治|邑治]]・[[巨勢小邑治|小邑治]]の父。[[冠位二十六階|冠位]]は[[小錦中]]・[[中納言]]だが、後述するように、官職については疑義がある。
== 考証 == その経歴や人生については、不明な点が多いが、『[[続日本紀]]』の[[巨勢邑治]]の薨伝<ref>『続日本紀』神亀元年6月6日条</ref>から、以下のことが分かる。 #彼の冠位である[[小錦中]]は、[[天智天皇]]3年（[[664]]）2月制定の[[冠位二十六階]]の第qq等であり、高位ではあった。しかし、[[天武天皇]]14年（[[685]]）正月には[[冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|冠位四十八階]]が制定されている。 #[[中納言]]は[[慶雲]]2年（[[705]]）4月制定の[[令外官]]であり、天智・天武朝には存在していない。天武朝になって設置され、「納言」に、後世になって「中」の文字を挿入したものと推定される<ref>[[青木和夫]]「[[田中卓]]氏の「中納言（その一）」を読む」『続日本紀研究』一 - 八</ref>。納言の文字は天武天皇9年（[[680]]）の[[舎人王]]や<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇下 9年7月25日条</ref>、持統天皇元年（[[687]]）正月の[[阿倍御主人|布勢御主人]]にも見え<ref>『日本書紀』持統天皇元年正月1日条</ref>、天武朝では大臣が置かれなかったために、納言が最高の行政官であった。納言の官名は中国由来のもので『[[書経]]』「舜典」に見えるもので、[[隋]]・[[唐]]の初期には[[門下省]]の長官（侍中）の称号であった。
以上のように、天武朝の高位・高官職の人物であり、天武天皇14年（[[685]]）までに亡くなっている。
== 系譜 == *父：巨勢徳多 *母：不詳 *生母不明の子女 **男子：[[巨勢邑治]] **男子：[[巨勢小邑治]]

== 考証 == その経歴や人生については、不明な点が多いが、『[[続日本紀]]』の[[巨勢邑治]]の薨伝<ref>『続日本紀』神亀元年6月6日条</ref>から、以下のことが分かる。 #彼の冠位である[[小錦中]]は、[[天智天皇]]3年（[[664]]）2月制定の[[冠位二十六階]]の第qq等であり、高位ではあった。しかし、[[天武天皇]]14年（[[685]]）正月には[[冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|冠位四十八階]]が制定されている。 #[[中納言]]は[[慶雲]]2年（[[705]]）4月制定の[[令外官]]であり、天智・天武朝には存在していない。天武朝になって設置され、「納言」に、後世になって「中」の文字を挿入したものと推定される<ref>[[青木和夫]]「[[田中卓]]氏の「中納言（その一）」を読む」『続日本紀研究』一 - 八</ref>。納言の文字は天武天皇9年（[[680]]）の[[舎人王]]や<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇下 9年7月25日条</ref>、持統天皇元年（[[687]]）正月の[[阿倍御主人|布勢御主人]]にも見え<ref>『日本書紀』持統天皇元年正月1日条</ref>、天武朝では大臣が置かれなかったために、納言が最高の行政官であった。納言の官名は中国由来のもので『[[書経]]』「舜典」に見えるもので、[[隋]]・[[唐]]の初期には[[門下省]]の長官（侍中）の称号であった。
以上のように、天武朝の高位・高官職の人物であり、天武天皇14年（[[685]]）までに亡くなっている。

== 考証 == その経歴や人生については、不明な点が多いが、『[[続日本紀]]』の[[巨勢邑治]]の薨伝<ref>『続日本紀』神亀元年6月6日条</ref>から、以下のことが分かる。 #彼の冠位である[[小錦中]]は、[[天智天皇]]3年（[[664]]）2月制定の[[冠位二十六階]]の第qq等であり、高位ではあった。しかし、[[天武天皇]]14年（[[685]]）正月には[[冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|冠位四十八階]]が制定されている。 #[[中納言]]は[[慶雲]]2年（[[705]]）4月制定の[[令外官]]であり、天智・天武朝には存在していない。天武朝になって設置され、「納言」に、後世になって「中」の文字を挿入したものと推定される<ref>[[青木和夫]]「[[田中卓]]氏の「中納言（その一）」を読む」『続日本紀研究』一 - 八</ref>。納言の文字は天武天皇9年（[[680]]）の[[舎人王]]や<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇下 9年7月25日条</ref>、持統天皇元年（[[687]]）正月の[[阿倍御主人|布勢御主人]]にも見え<ref>『日本書紀』持統天皇元年正月1日条</ref>、天武朝では大臣が置かれなかったために、納言が最高の行政官であった。納言の官名は中国由来のもので『[[書経]]』「舜典」に見えるもので、[[隋]]・[[唐]]の初期には[[門下省]]の長官（侍中）の称号であった。
以上のように、天武朝の高位・高官職の人物であり、天武天皇14年（[[685]]）までに亡くなっている。
== 系譜 == *父：巨勢徳多 *母：不詳 *生母不明の子女 **男子：[[巨勢邑治]] **男子：[[巨勢小邑治]]

[[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kose no Kurohito}}[[Category:11]] <!--Kose no Kurohito--> [[ja:巨勢黒麻呂]] [[Category:Pages to be translated]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]]{{citation needed|Kose no Kurohito}}[[Category:11]]
[[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]]{{citation needed|Kose no Kurohito}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kose no Kurohito}}[[Category:11]] <!--Kose no Kurohito--> [[ja:冠位・位階制度の変遷]]
[[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]]{{citation needed|Kose no Kurohito}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kose no Kurohito}}[[Category:11]] <!--Kose no Kurohito--> [[Category:Shōkinchū]][[Category:Japanese court rank system]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shōkinchū]][[Category:Shōkinchū forks]] [[Category:Kose no Kurohito]]
[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]
[[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:ose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] '''巨勢 黒麻呂'''（こせ の くろまろ）は、[[飛鳥時代]]の[[貴族]]。名は'''黒丸'''とも記される<ref>『公卿補任』天平9年巨勢朝臣堺麿の条</ref>。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[臣]]。[[左大臣]]・[[巨勢徳多]]の子で、[[巨勢邑治|邑治]]・[[巨勢小邑治|小邑治]]の父。[[冠位二十六階|冠位]]は[[小錦中]]・[[中納言]]だが、後述するように、官職については疑義がある。
== 考証 == その経歴や人生については、不明な点が多いが、『[[続日本紀]]』の[[巨勢邑治]]の薨伝<ref>『続日本紀』神亀元年6月6日条</ref>から、以下のことが分かる。 #彼の冠位である[[小錦中]]は、[[天智天皇]]3年（[[664]]）2月制定の[[冠位二十六階]]の第11等であり、高位ではあった。しかし、[[天武天皇]]14年（[[685]]）正月には[[冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|冠位四十八階]]が制定されている。 #[[中納言]]は[[慶雲]]2年（[[705]]）4月制定の[[令外官]]であり、天智・天武朝には存在していない。天武朝になって設置され、「納言」に、後世になって「中」の文字を挿入したものと推定される<ref>[[青木和夫]]「[[田中卓]]氏の「中納言（その一）」を読む」『続日本紀研究』一 - 八</ref>。納言の文字は天武天皇9年（[[680]]）の[[舎人王]]や<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇下 9年7月25日条</ref>、持統天皇元年（[[687]]）正月の[[阿倍御主人|布勢御主人]]にも見え<ref>『日本書紀』持統天皇元年正月1日条</ref>、天武朝では大臣が置かれなかったために、納言が最高の行政官であった。納言の官名は中国由来のもので『[[書経]]』「舜典」に見えるもので、[[隋]]・[[唐]]の初期には[[門下省]]の長官（侍中）の称号であった。
以上のように、天武朝の高位・高官職の人物であり、天武天皇14年（[[685]]）までに亡くなっている。
== 系譜 == *父：巨勢徳多 *母：不詳 *生母不明の子女 **男子：[[巨勢邑治]] **男子：[[巨勢小邑治]]

[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]
[[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:ose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] '''Kose no Kuromaro''' (巨勢 黒麻呂) is an aristocrat of the {{ill|Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. His name is also recorded as '''Kuromaru'''. His {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ|lt=Surname|lt_ja=姓}} is of the type {{ill|Subject|ja|臣}}. He was the son of the {{ill|Left Minister|ja|左大臣}} {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多}}, and the father of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治}} and {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治}}.<ref>'Kōkei Honin': Clause of Kose no Asomi Sakaimaro in Tenpyō 9</ref> His court rank, as indicated by the {{ill|26‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位二十六階|lt=Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位}}, was that of {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中}} and {{ill|Chunagon|ja|中納言}}, though there are doubts regarding his official positions.
== Analysis == Although many details of his career and life remain unclear, from the obituary of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治}} in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}<ref>'Shoku Nihongi': Kamiki 1, June 6 entry</ref> the following can be ascertained: # His court rank, {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中}}, was the 11th rank of the 26‐step system established in February of the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}’s reign ({{ill|664|ja|664}}), and although it was a high rank, by January of the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}’s reign ({{ill|685|ja|685}}) the {{ill|48‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|lt=48‐step Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位四十八階}} was established. # {{ill|Chunagon|ja|中納言}} was established in April of the 2nd year of {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} ({{ill|705|ja|705}}) under the title of {{ill|Reigai-kan|ja|令外官}}, a position that did not exist during the Tenji and Tenmu periods. It is presumed that once established during the Tenmu period the character “中” was later inserted into the title “納言”.<ref>{{ill|Kazuo Aoki|ja|青木和夫}}: "Reading {{ill|Taku Tanaka|ja|田中卓}}’s ‘Chunagon (Part 1)’" in ''Shoku Nihongi Kenkyū Ichi – Hachi''</ref> The title “Nagon” appears in instances such as in the 9th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} ({{ill|680|ja|680}}) with {{ill|Toneri-ō|ja|舎人王}},<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Emperor Tenmu, 9th year, July 25 entry</ref> and in January of the 1st year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} ({{ill|687|ja|687}}) with {{ill|Abe Goshujin|ja|阿倍御主人|lt=Fuse Goshujin|lt_ja=布勢御主人}}.<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Empress Jitō, January 1 entry</ref> Since no ministers were appointed during the Tenmu period, the position of Nagon was the highest administrative office. The title “Nagon” is of Chinese origin, as seen in the "Shuntian" of the {{ill|Shu Jing|ja|書経}}, and in the early Sui and Tang dynasties it was the title for the head ({{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍中}}) of the {{ill|Monkashō|ja|門下省}}.
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多}} * Mother: Unknown * Children by an unknown consort: ** Son: {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治}} ** Son: {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治}}
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * 『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}』 (Volume 5), {{ill|Iwanami Bunko|ja|岩波文庫}}, 1995 * 『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}』 Complete Modern Translation (Lower Volume), {{ill|Kodansha Academic Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, translated by {{ill|Mou Ujiyatani|ja|宇治谷孟}}, 1988 * 『{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}』 Vol. 2, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店}}, 1990 * {{ill|Mou Ujiyatani|ja|宇治谷孟}} 『Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)』, {{ill|Kodansha Academic Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, 1992

[[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:ose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] '''Kose no Kuromaro''' (巨勢 黒麻呂) is an aristocrat of the {{ill|Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. His name is also recorded as '''Kuromaru'''. His {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ|lt=Surname|lt_ja=姓}} is of the type {{ill|Subject|ja|臣}}. He was the son of the {{ill|Left Minister|ja|左大臣}} {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多}}, and the father of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治}} and {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治}}.<ref>'Kōkei Honin': Clause of Kose no Asomi Sakaimaro in Tenpyō 9</ref> His court rank, as indicated by the {{ill|26‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位二十六階|lt=Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位}}, was that of {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中}} and {{ill|Chunagon|ja|中納言}}, though there are doubts regarding his official positions.
== Analysis == Although many details of his career and life remain unclear, from the obituary of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治}} in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}<ref>'Shoku Nihongi': Kamiki 1, June 6 entry</ref> the following can be ascertained: # His court rank, {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中}}, was the 11th rank of the 26‐step system established in February of the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}’s reign ({{ill|664|ja|664}}), and although it was a high rank, by January of the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}’s reign ({{ill|685|ja|685}}) the {{ill|48‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|lt=48‐step Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位四十八階}} was established. # {{ill|Chunagon|ja|中納言}} was established in April of the 2nd year of {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} ({{ill|705|ja|705}}) under the title of {{ill|Reigai-kan|ja|令外官}}, a position that did not exist during the Tenji and Tenmu periods. It is presumed that once established during the Tenmu period the character “中” was later inserted into the title “納言”.<ref>{{ill|Kazuo Aoki|ja|青木和夫}}: "Reading {{ill|Taku Tanaka|ja|田中卓}}’s ‘Chunagon (Part 1)’" in ''Shoku Nihongi Kenkyū Ichi – Hachi''</ref> The title “Nagon” appears in instances such as in the 9th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} ({{ill|680|ja|680}}) with {{ill|Toneri-ō|ja|舎人王}},<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Emperor Tenmu, 9th year, July 25 entry</ref> and in January of the 1st year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} ({{ill|687|ja|687}}) with {{ill|Abe Goshujin|ja|阿倍御主人|lt=Fuse Goshujin|lt_ja=布勢御主人}}.<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Empress Jitō, January 1 entry</ref> Since no ministers were appointed during the Tenmu period, the position of Nagon was the highest administrative office. The title “Nagon” is of Chinese origin, as seen in the "Shuntian" of the {{ill|Shu Jing|ja|書経}}, and in the early Sui and Tang dynasties it was the title for the head ({{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍中}}) of the {{ill|Monkashō|ja|門下省}}.
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多}} * Mother: Unknown * Children by an unknown consort: ** Son: {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治}} ** Son: {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治}}
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * 『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}』 (Volume 5), {{ill|Iwanami Bunko|ja|岩波文庫}}, 1995 * 『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}』 Complete Modern Translation (Lower Volume), {{ill|Kodansha Academic Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, translated by {{ill|Mou Ujiyatani|ja|宇治谷孟}}, 1988 * 『{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}』 Vol. 2, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店}}, 1990 * {{ill|Mou Ujiyatani|ja|宇治谷孟}} 『Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)』, {{ill|Kodansha Academic Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, 1992
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{people-stub}} {{citation needed|こせ の くろまろ}}

[[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:ose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] '''Kose no Kuromaro''' (巨勢 黒麻呂) is an aristocrat of the {{ill|Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. His name is also recorded as '''Kuromaru'''. His {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ|lt=Surname|lt_ja=姓}} is of the type {{ill|Subject|ja|臣}}. He was the son of the {{ill|Left Minister|ja|左大臣}} {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多}}, and the father of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治}} and {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治}}.<ref>'Kōkei Honin': Clause of Kose no Asomi Sakaimaro in Tenpyō 9</ref> His court rank, as indicated by the {{ill|26‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位二十六階|lt=Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位}}, was that of {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中}} and {{ill|Chunagon|ja|中納言}}, though there are doubts regarding his official positions.
== Analysis == Although many details of his career and life remain unclear, from the obituary of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治}} in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}<ref>'Shoku Nihongi': Kamiki 1, June 6 entry</ref> the following can be ascertained: # His court rank, {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中}}, was the 11th rank of the 26‐step system established in February of the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}’s reign ({{ill|664|ja|664}}), and although it was a high rank, by January of the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}’s reign ({{ill|685|ja|685}}) the {{ill|48‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|lt=48‐step Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位四十八階}} was established. # {{ill|Chunagon|ja|中納言}} was established in April of the 2nd year of {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} ({{ill|705|ja|705}}) under the title of {{ill|Reigai-kan|ja|令外官}}, a position that did not exist during the Tenji and Tenmu periods. It is presumed that once established during the Tenmu period the character “中” was later inserted into the title “納言”.<ref>{{ill|Kazuo Aoki|ja|青木和夫}}: "Reading {{ill|Taku Tanaka|ja|田中卓}}’s ‘Chunagon (Part 1)’" in ''Shoku Nihongi Kenkyū Ichi – Hachi''</ref> The title “Nagon” appears in instances such as in the 9th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} ({{ill|680|ja|680}}) with {{ill|Toneri-ō|ja|舎人王}},<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Emperor Tenmu, 9th year, July 25 entry</ref> and in January of the 1st year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} ({{ill|687|ja|687}}) with {{ill|Abe Goshujin|ja|阿倍御主人|lt=Fuse Goshujin|lt_ja=布勢御主人}}.<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Empress Jitō, January 1 entry</ref> Since no ministers were appointed during the Tenmu period, the position of Nagon was the highest administrative office. The title “Nagon” is of Chinese origin, as seen in the "Shuntian" of the {{ill|Shu Jing|ja|書経}}, and in the early Sui and Tang dynasties it was the title for the head ({{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍中}}) of the {{ill|Monkashō|ja|門下省}}.
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多}} * Mother: Unknown * Children by an unknown consort: ** Son: {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治}} ** Son: {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治}}
== References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * 『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}』 (Volume 5), {{ill|Iwanami Bunko|ja|岩波文庫}}, 1995 * 『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}』 Complete Modern Translation (Lower Volume), {{ill|Kodansha Academic Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, translated by {{ill|Mou Ujiyatani|ja|宇治谷孟}}, 1988 * 『{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}』 Vol. 2, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店}}, 1990 * {{ill|Mou Ujiyatani|ja|宇治谷孟}} 『Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)』, {{ill|Kodansha Academic Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, 1992
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{people-stub}} {{citation needed|こせ の くろまろ}}

[[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:ose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kose no Kurohito]][[Category:11]] '''Kose no Kuromaro''' (巨勢 黒麻呂) is an aristocrat of the {{ill|Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. His name is also recorded as '''Kuromaru'''. His {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ|lt=Surname|lt_ja=姓}} is of the type {{ill|Subject|ja|臣}}. He was the son of the {{ill|Left Minister|ja|左大臣}} {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多}}, and the father of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治}} and {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治}}.<ref>'Kōkei Honin': Clause of Kose no Asomi Sakaimaro in Tenpyō 9</ref> His court rank, as indicated by the {{ill|26‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位二十六階|lt=Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位}}, was that of {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中}} and {{ill|Chunagon|ja|中納言}}, though there are doubts regarding his official positions.
== Analysis == Although many details of his career and life remain unclear, from the obituary of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治}} in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}<ref>'Shoku Nihongi': Kamiki 1, June 6 entry</ref> the following can be ascertained: # His court rank, {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中}}, was the 11th rank of the 26‐step system established in February of the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}’s reign ({{ill|664|ja|664}}), and although it was a high rank, by January of the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}’s reign ({{ill|685|ja|685}}) the {{ill|48‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|lt=48‐step Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位四十八階}} was established. # {{ill|Chunagon|ja|中納言}} was established in April of the 2nd year of {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} ({{ill|705|ja|705}}) under the title of {{ill|Reigai-kan|ja|令外官}}, a position that did not exist during the Tenji and Tenmu periods. It is presumed that once established during the Tenmu period the character “中” was later inserted into the title “納言”.<ref>{{ill|Kazuo Aoki|ja|青木和夫}}: "Reading {{ill|Taku Tanaka|ja|田中卓}}’s ‘Chunagon (Part 1)’" in ''Shoku Nihongi Kenkyū Ichi – Hachi''</ref> The title “Nagon” appears in instances such as in the 9th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} ({{ill|680|ja|680}}) with {{ill|Toneri-ō|ja|舎人王}},<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Emperor Tenmu, 9th year, July 25 entry</ref> and in January of the 1st year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} ({{ill|687|ja|687}}) with {{ill|Abe Goshujin|ja|阿倍御主人|lt=Fuse Goshujin|lt_ja=布勢御主人}}.<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Empress Jitō, January 1 entry</ref> Since no ministers were appointed during the Tenmu period, the position of Nagon was the highest administrative office. The title “Nagon” is of Chinese origin, as seen in the "Shuntian" of the {{ill|Shu Jing|ja|書経}}, and in the early Sui and Tang dynasties it was the title for the head ({{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍中}}) of the {{ill|Monkashō|ja|門下省}}.
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多}} * Mother: Unknown * Children by an unknown consort: ** Son: {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治}} ** Son: {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * 『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}』 (Volume 5), {{ill|Iwanami Bunko|ja|岩波文庫}}, 1995 * 『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}』 Complete Modern Translation (Lower Volume), {{ill|Kodansha Academic Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, translated by {{ill|Mou Ujiyatani|ja|宇治谷孟}}, 1988 * 『{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}』 Vol. 2, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店}}, 1990 * {{ill|Mou Ujiyatani|ja|宇治谷孟}} 『Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)』, {{ill|Kodansha Academic Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫}}, 1992

'''Kose no Kuromaro''' (巨勢 黒麻呂) is an aristocrat of the {{ill|Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代}}. His name is also recorded as '''Kuromaru'''. His {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ|lt=Surname|lt_ja=姓}} is of the type {{ill|Subject|ja|臣}}. He was the son of the {{ill|Left Minister|ja|左大臣}} {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多}}, and the father of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治}} and {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治}}.<ref>'Kōkei Honin': Clause of Kose no Asomi Sakaimaro in Tenpyō 9</ref> His court rank, as indicated by the {{ill|26‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位二十六階|lt=Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位}}, was that of {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中}} and {{ill|Chunagon|ja|中納言}}, though there are doubts regarding his official positions.
== Analysis == Although many details of his career and life remain unclear, from the obituary of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治}} in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}<ref>'Shoku Nihongi': Kamiki 1, June 6 entry</ref> the following can be ascertained: # His court rank, {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中}}, was the 11th rank of the 26‐step system established in February of the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇}}’s reign ({{ill|664|ja|664}}), and although it was a high rank, by January of the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}’s reign ({{ill|685|ja|685}}) the {{ill|48‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|lt=48‐step Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位四十八階}} was established. # {{ill|Chunagon|ja|中納言}} was established in April of the 2nd year of {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲}} ({{ill|705|ja|705}}) under the title of {{ill|Reigai-kan|ja|令外官}}, a position that did not exist during the Tenji and Tenmu periods. It is presumed that once established during the Tenmu period the character “中” was later inserted into the title “納言”.<ref>{{ill|Kazuo Aoki|ja|青木和夫}}: "Reading {{ill|Taku Tanaka|ja|田中卓}}’s ‘Chunagon (Part 1)’" in ''Shoku Nihongi Kenkyū Ichi – Hachi''</ref> The title “Nagon” appears in instances such as in the 9th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} ({{ill|680|ja|680}}) with {{ill|Toneri-ō|ja|舎人王}},<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Emperor Tenmu, 9th year, July 25 entry</ref> and in January of the 1st year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} ({{ill|687|ja|687}}) with {{ill|Abe Goshujin|ja|阿倍御主人|lt=Fuse Goshujin|lt_ja=布勢御主人}}.<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Empress Jitō, January 1 entry</ref> Since no ministers were appointed during the Tenmu period, the position of Nagon was the highest administrative office. The title “Nagon” is of Chinese origin, as seen in the "Shuntian" of the {{ill|Shu Jing|ja|書経}}, and in the early Sui and Tang dynasties it was the title for the head ({{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍中}}) of the {{ill|Monkashō|ja|門下省}}.
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多}} * Mother: Unknown * Children by an unknown consort: ** Son: {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治}} ** Son: {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}

His {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ|lt=Surname|lt_ja=姓|12=draft}} is of the type {{ill|Subject|ja|臣|12=draft}}. He was the son of the {{ill|Left Minister|ja|左大臣|12=draft}} {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|12=draft}}, and the father of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|12=draft}}.<ref>'Kōkei Honin': Clause of Kose no Asomi Sakaimaro in Tenpyō 9</ref> His court rank, as indicated by the {{ill|26‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位二十六階|lt=Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位|12=draft}}, was that of {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|12=draft}} and {{ill|Chunagon|ja|中納言|12=draft}}, though there are doubts regarding his official positions.
== Analysis == Although many details of his career and life remain unclear, from the obituary of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft}}<ref>'Shoku Nihongi': Kamiki 1, June 6 entry</ref> the following can be ascertained: # His court rank, {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|12=draft}}, was the 11th rank of the 26‐step system established in February of the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft}}’s reign ({{ill|664|ja|664|12=draft}}), and although it was a high rank, by January of the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}}’s reign ({{ill|685|ja|685|12=draft}}) the {{ill|48‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|lt=48‐step Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位四十八階|12=draft}} was established. # {{ill|Chunagon|ja|中納言|12=draft}} was established in April of the 2nd year of {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲|12=draft}} ({{ill|705|ja|705|12=draft}}) under the title of {{ill|Reigai-kan|ja|令外官|12=draft}}, a position that did not exist during the Tenji and Tenmu periods. It is presumed that once established during the Tenmu period the character “中” was later inserted into the title “納言”.<ref>{{ill|Kazuo Aoki|ja|青木和夫|12=draft}}: "Reading {{ill|Taku Tanaka|ja|田中卓|12=draft}}’s ‘Chunagon (Part 1)’" in ''Shoku Nihongi Kenkyū Ichi – Hachi''</ref> The title “Nagon” appears in instances such as in the 9th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}} ({{ill|680|ja|680|12=draft}}) with {{ill|Toneri-ō|ja|舎人王|12=draft}},<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Emperor Tenmu, 9th year, July 25 entry</ref> and in January of the 1st year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}} ({{ill|687|ja|687|12=draft}}) with {{ill|Abe Goshujin|ja|阿倍御主人|lt=Fuse Goshujin|lt_ja=布勢御主人|12=draft}}.<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Empress Jitō, January 1 entry</ref> Since no ministers were appointed during the Tenmu period, the position of Nagon was the highest administrative office. The title “Nagon” is of Chinese origin, as seen in the "Shuntian" of the {{ill|Shu Jing|ja|書経|12=draft}}, and in the early Sui and Tang dynasties it was the title for the head ({{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍中|12=draft}}) of the {{ill|Monkashō|ja|門下省|12=draft}}.
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|12=draft}} * Mother: Unknown * Children by an unknown consort: ** Son: {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=draft}} ** Son: {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|12=draft}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}

'''Kose no Kuromaro''' (巨勢 黒麻呂) is an aristocrat of the {{ill|Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代|12=draft}}. His name is also recorded as '''Kuromaru'''. His {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ|lt=Surname|lt_ja=姓|12=draft}} is of the type {{ill|Subject|ja|臣|12=draft}}. He was the son of the {{ill|Left Minister|ja|左大臣|12=draft}} {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|12=draft}}, and the father of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|12=draft}}.<ref>'Kōkei Honin': Clause of Kose no Asomi Sakaimaro in Tenpyō 9</ref> His court rank, as indicated by the {{ill|26‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位二十六階|lt=Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位|12=draft}}, was that of {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|12=draft}} and {{ill|Chunagon|ja|中納言|12=draft}}, though there are doubts regarding his official positions.
== Analysis == Although many details of his career and life remain unclear, from the obituary of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft}}<ref>'Shoku Nihongi': Kamiki 1, June 6 entry</ref> the following can be ascertained: # His court rank, {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|12=draft}}, was the 11th rank of the 26‐step system established in February of the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|ja|天智天皇|12=draft}}’s reign ({{ill|664|ja|664|12=draft}}), and although it was a high rank, by January of the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}}’s reign ({{ill|685|ja|685|12=draft}}) the {{ill|48‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|lt=48‐step Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位四十八階|12=draft}} was established. # {{ill|Chunagon|ja|中納言|12=draft}} was established in April of the 2nd year of {{ill|Keiun|ja|慶雲|12=draft}} ({{ill|705|ja|705|12=draft}}) under the title of {{ill|Reigai-kan|ja|令外官|12=draft}}, a position that did not exist during the Tenji and Tenmu periods. It is presumed that once established during the Tenmu period the character “中” was later inserted into the title “納言”.<ref>{{ill|Kazuo Aoki|ja|青木和夫|12=draft}}: "Reading {{ill|Taku Tanaka|ja|田中卓|12=draft}}’s ‘Chunagon (Part 1)’" in ''Shoku Nihongi Kenkyū Ichi – Hachi''</ref> The title “Nagon” appears in instances such as in the 9th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}} ({{ill|680|ja|680|12=draft}}) with {{ill|Toneri-ō|ja|舎人王|12=draft}},<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Emperor Tenmu, 9th year, July 25 entry</ref> and in January of the 1st year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}} ({{ill|687|ja|687|12=draft}}) with {{ill|Abe Goshujin|ja|阿倍御主人|lt=Fuse Goshujin|lt_ja=布勢御主人|12=draft}}.<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Empress Jitō, January 1 entry</ref> Since no ministers were appointed during the Tenmu period, the position of Nagon was the highest administrative office. The title “Nagon” is of Chinese origin, as seen in the "Shuntian" of the {{ill|Shu Jing|ja|書経|12=draft}}, and in the early Sui and Tang dynasties it was the title for the head ({{ill|Chamberlain|ja|侍中|12=draft}}) of the {{ill|Monkashō|ja|門下省|12=draft}}.

6 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:巨勢黒麻呂 up to rev 93352506
revision.text:
'''巨勢 黒麻呂'''（こせ の くろまろ）は、[[飛鳥時代]]の[[貴族]]。名は'''黒丸'''とも記される<ref>『公卿補任』天平9年巨勢朝臣堺麿の条</ref>。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[臣]]。[[左大臣]]・[[巨勢徳多]]の子で、[[巨勢邑治|邑治]]・[[巨勢小邑治|小邑治]]の父。[[冠位二十六階|冠位]]は[[小錦中]]・[[中納言]]だが、後述するように、官職については疑義がある。
== 考証 == その経歴や人生については、不明な点が多いが、『[[続日本紀]]』の[[巨勢邑治]]の薨伝<ref>『続日本紀』神亀元年6月6日条</ref>から、以下のことが分かる。 #彼の冠位である[[小錦中]]は、[[天智天皇]]3年（[[664年]]）2月制定の[[冠位二十六階]]の第11等であり、高位ではあった。しかし、[[天武天皇]]14年（[[685年]]）正月には[[冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|冠位四十八階]]が制定されている。 #[[中納言]]は[[慶雲]]2年（[[705年]]）4月制定の[[令外官]]であり、天智・天武朝には存在していない。天武朝になって設置され、「納言」に、後世になって「中」の文字を挿入したものと推定される<ref>[[青木和夫]]「[[田中卓]]氏の「中納言（その一）」を読む」『続日本紀研究』一 - 八</ref>。納言の文字は天武天皇9年（[[680年]]）の[[舎人王]]や<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇下 9年7月25日条</ref>、持統天皇元年（[[687年]]）正月の[[阿倍御主人|布勢御主人]]にも見え<ref>『日本書紀』持統天皇元年正月1日条</ref>、天武朝では大臣が置かれなかったために、納言が最高の行政官であった。納言の官名は中国由来のもので『[[書経]]』「舜典」に見えるもので、[[隋]]・[[唐]]の初期には[[門下省]]の長官（侍中）の称号であった。
以上のように、天武朝の高位・高官職の人物であり、天武天皇14年（[[685年]]）までに亡くなっている。
== 系譜 == *父：巨勢徳多 *母：不詳 *生母不明の子女 **男子：[[巨勢邑治]] **男子：[[巨勢小邑治]]
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『日本書紀』(五)、[[岩波文庫]]、1995年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳(下）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年 * 『続日本紀』2 新日本古典文学大系13[[岩波書店]]、1990年 * [[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社学術文庫]]、1992年
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{people-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こせ の くろまろ}} [[Category:巨勢氏|くろまろ]] [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物]] [[Category:7世紀生]] [[Category:7世紀没]]

revision.text:

以上のように、天武朝の高位・高官職の人物であり、天武天皇14年（[[685年]]）までに亡くなっている。

== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『日本書紀』(五)、[[岩波文庫]]、1995年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳(下）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年 * 『続日本紀』2 新日本古典文学大系13[[岩波書店]]、1990年 * [[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （上）』[[講談社学術文庫]]、1992年

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢黒麻呂]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q109287613]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q109287613]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Kose no Kuromaro''' (巨勢 黒麻呂) is an aristocrat of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka Period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Kuromaru'''. His [[:en:Kabane|Surname]] is of the type [[:en:Omi (title)|Subject]]. He was the son of the [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|qq=|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}}, and the father of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|qq=|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} and {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kooharu|qq=|1=Kose Kooharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162107}}.<ref>'Kōkei Honin': Clause of Kose no Asomi Sakaimaro in Tenpyō 9</ref> His court Rank, as indicated by the {{ill|26‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位二十六階|lt=Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26‐step Court Rank|qq=|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209277}}, was that of {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshikinchu|qq=|1=Shōkinchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464366}} and [[:en:Chūnagon|Chunagon]], though there are doubts regarding his official positions.
== Analysis == Although many details of his career and life remain unclear, from the obituary of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|qq=|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]<ref>'Shoku Nihongi': Kamiki 1, June 6 entry</ref> the following can be ascertained: # His court Rank, {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshikinchu|qq=|1=Shōkinchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464366}}, was the 11th Rank of the 26‐step system established in February of the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]’s reign ([[:en:664|664]]), and although it was a high Rank, by January of the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}’s reign ([[:en:685|685]]) the {{ill|48‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|lt=48‐step Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位四十八階|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/48‐step Court Rank|qq=|1=history of court rank systems in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395032}} was established. # [[:en:Chūnagon|Chunagon]] was established in April of the 2nd year of [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]] ([[:en:705|705]]) under the title of {{ill|Reigai-kan|ja|令外官|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reigai-kan|qq=|1=East Asian extra-statutory office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378315}}, a position that did not exist during the Tenji and Tenmu periods. It is presumed that once established during the Tenmu period the character “中” was later inserted into the title “納言”.<ref>{{ill|Kazuo Aoki|ja|青木和夫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuo Aoki|qq=|1=Kazuo Aoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661670}}: "Reading {{ill|Taku Tanaka|ja|田中卓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taku Tanaka|qq=|1=Takashi Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575370}}’s ‘Chunagon (Part 1)’" in ''Shoku Nihongi Kenkyū Ichi – Hachi''</ref> The title “Nagon” appears in instances such as in the 9th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ([[:en:680|680]]) with {{ill|Toneri-ō|ja|舎人王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toneri-ō|qq=|1=Toneri no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65279725}},<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Emperor Tenmu, 9th year, July 25 entry</ref> and in January of the 1st year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] ([[:en:687|687]]) with {{ill|Abe Goshujin|ja|阿倍御主人|lt=Fuse Goshujin|lt_ja=布勢御主人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Goshujin|qq=|1=Abe no Miushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657245}}.<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Empress Jitō, January 1 entry</ref> Since no ministers were appointed during the Tenmu period, the position of Nagon was the highest administrative office. The title “Nagon” is of Chinese origin, as seen in the "Shuntian" of the [[:en:Book of Documents|Shu Jing]], and in the early Sui and Tang dynasties it was the title for the head ([[:en:Palace Attendant|Chamberlain]]) of the [[:en:Menxia Sheng|Monkashō]].
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|qq=|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}} * Mother: Unknown * Children by an unknown consort: ** Son: {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|qq=|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} ** Son: {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kooharu|qq=|1=Kose Kooharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162107}}

He was the son of the [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|qq=|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}}, and the father of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|qq=|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} and {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kooharu|qq=|1=Kose Kooharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162107}}.<ref>'Kōkei Honin': Clause of Kose no Asomi Sakaimaro in Tenpyō 9</ref> His court Rank, as indicated by the {{ill|26‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位二十六階|lt=Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26‐step Court Rank|qq=|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209277}}, was that of {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshikinchu|qq=|1=Shōkinchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464366}} and [[:en:Chūnagon|Chunagon]], though there are doubts regarding his official positions.

== Analysis == Although many details of his career and life remain unclear, from the obituary of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|qq=|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]<ref>'Shoku Nihongi': Kamiki 1, June 6 entry</ref> the following can be ascertained: # His court Rank, {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshikinchu|qq=|1=Shōkinchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464366}}, was the 11th Rank of the 26‐step system established in February of the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]’s reign ([[:en:664|664]]), and although it was a high Rank, by January of the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}’s reign ([[:en:685|685]]) the {{ill|48‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|lt=48‐step Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位四十八階|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/48‐step Court Rank|qq=|1=history of court rank systems in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395032}} was established. # [[:en:Chūnagon|Chunagon]] was established in April of the 2nd year of [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]] ([[:en:705|705]]) under the title of {{ill|Reigai-kan|ja|令外官|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reigai-kan|qq=|1=East Asian extra-statutory office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378315}}, a position that did not exist during the Tenji and Tenmu periods. It is presumed that once established during the Tenmu period the character “中” was later inserted into the title “納言”.<ref>{{ill|Kazuo Aoki|ja|青木和夫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuo Aoki|qq=|1=Kazuo Aoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661670}}: "Reading {{ill|Taku Tanaka|ja|田中卓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taku Tanaka|qq=|1=Takashi Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575370}}’s ‘Chunagon (Part 1)’" in ''Shoku Nihongi Kenkyū Ichi – Hachi''</ref> The title “Nagon” appears in instances such as in the 9th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ([[:en:680|680]]) with {{ill|Toneri-ō|ja|舎人王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toneri-ō|qq=|1=Toneri no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65279725}},<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Emperor Tenmu, 9th year, July 25 entry</ref> and in January of the 1st year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] ([[:en:687|687]]) with {{ill|Abe Goshujin|ja|阿倍御主人|lt=Fuse Goshujin|lt_ja=布勢御主人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Goshujin|qq=|1=Abe no Miushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657245}}.<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Empress Jitō, January 1 entry</ref> Since no ministers were appointed during the Tenmu period, the position of Nagon was the highest administrative office. The title “Nagon” is of Chinese origin, as seen in the "Shuntian" of the [[:en:Book of Documents|Shu Jing]], and in the early Sui and Tang dynasties it was the title for the head ([[:en:Palace Attendant|Chamberlain]]) of the [[:en:Menxia Sheng|Monkashō]].
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|qq=|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}} * Mother: Unknown * Children by an unknown consort: ** Son: {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|qq=|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} ** Son: {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kooharu|qq=|1=Kose Kooharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162107}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢黒麻呂]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q109287613]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q109287613]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Kose no Kuromaro''' (巨勢 黒麻呂) is an aristocrat of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka Period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Kuromaru'''. His [[:en:Kabane|Surname]] is of the type [[:en:Omi (title)|Subject]]. He was the son of the [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}}, and the father of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} and {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kooharu|1=Kose Kooharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162107}}.<ref>'Kōkei Honin': Clause of Kose no Asomi Sakaimaro in Tenpyō 9</ref> His court Rank, as indicated by the {{ill|26‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位二十六階|lt=Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26‐step Court Rank|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209277}}, was that of {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshikinchu|1=Shōkinchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464366}} and [[:en:Chūnagon|Chunagon]], though there are doubts regarding his official positions.
== Analysis == Although many details of his career and life remain unclear, from the obituary of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]<ref>'Shoku Nihongi': Kamiki 1, June 6 entry</ref> the following can be ascertained: # His court Rank, {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshikinchu|1=Shōkinchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464366}}, was the 11th Rank of the 26‐step system established in February of the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]’s reign ([[:en:664|664]]), and although it was a high Rank, by January of the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}’s reign ([[:en:685|685]]) the {{ill|48‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|lt=48‐step Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位四十八階|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/48‐step Court Rank|1=history of court rank systems in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395032}} was established. # [[:en:Chūnagon|Chunagon]] was established in April of the 2nd year of [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]] ([[:en:705|705]]) under the title of {{ill|Reigai-kan|ja|令外官|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reigai-kan|1=East Asian extra-statutory office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378315}}, a position that did not exist during the Tenji and Tenmu periods. It is presumed that once established during the Tenmu period the character “中” was later inserted into the title “納言”.<ref>{{ill|Kazuo Aoki|ja|青木和夫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuo Aoki|1=Kazuo Aoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661670}}: "Reading {{ill|Taku Tanaka|ja|田中卓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taku Tanaka|1=Takashi Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575370}}’s ‘Chunagon (Part 1)’" in ''Shoku Nihongi Kenkyū Ichi – Hachi''</ref> The title “Nagon” appears in instances such as in the 9th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ([[:en:680|680]]) with {{ill|Toneri-ō|ja|舎人王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toneri-ō|1=Toneri no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65279725}},<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Emperor Tenmu, 9th year, July 25 entry</ref> and in January of the 1st year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] ([[:en:687|687]]) with {{ill|Abe Goshujin|ja|阿倍御主人|lt=Fuse Goshujin|lt_ja=布勢御主人|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Goshujin|1=Abe no Miushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657245}}.<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Empress Jitō, January 1 entry</ref> Since no ministers were appointed during the Tenmu period, the position of Nagon was the highest administrative office. The title “Nagon” is of Chinese origin, as seen in the "Shuntian" of the [[:en:Book of Documents|Shu Jing]], and in the early Sui and Tang dynasties it was the title for the head ([[:en:Palace Attendant|Chamberlain]]) of the [[:en:Menxia Sheng|Monkashō]].

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢黒麻呂]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q109287613]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q109287613]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Kose no Kuromaro''' (巨勢 黒麻呂) is an aristocrat of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka Period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Kuromaru'''. His [[:en:Kabane|Surname]] is of the type [[:en:Omi (title)|Subject]]. He was the son of the [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}}, and the father of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} and {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kooharu|1=Kose Kooharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162107}}.<ref>'Kōkei Honin': Clause of Kose no Asomi Sakaimaro in Tenpyō 9</ref> His court Rank, as indicated by the {{ill|26‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位二十六階|lt=Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26‐step Court Rank|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209277}}, was that of {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshikinchu|1=Shōkinchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464366}} and [[:en:Chūnagon|Chunagon]], though there are doubts regarding his official positions.
== Analysis == Although many details of his career and life remain unclear, from the obituary of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]<ref>'Shoku Nihongi': Kamiki 1, June 6 entry</ref> the following can be ascertained: # His court Rank, {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshikinchu|1=Shōkinchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464366}}, was the 11th Rank of the 26‐step system established in February of the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]’s reign ([[:en:664|664]]), and although it was a high Rank, by January of the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}’s reign ([[:en:685|685]]) the {{ill|48‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|lt=48‐step Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位四十八階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/48‐step Court Rank|1=history of court rank systems in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395032}} was established. # [[:en:Chūnagon|Chunagon]] was established in April of the 2nd year of [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]] ([[:en:705|705]]) under the title of {{ill|Reigai-kan|ja|令外官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reigai-kan|1=East Asian extra-statutory office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378315}}, a position that did not exist during the Tenji and Tenmu periods. It is presumed that once established during the Tenmu period the character “中” was later inserted into the title “納言”.<ref>{{ill|Kazuo Aoki|ja|青木和夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuo Aoki|1=Kazuo Aoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661670}}: "Reading {{ill|Taku Tanaka|ja|田中卓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taku Tanaka|1=Takashi Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575370}}’s ‘Chunagon (Part 1)’" in ''Shoku Nihongi Kenkyū Ichi – Hachi''</ref> The title “Nagon” appears in instances such as in the 9th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ([[:en:680|680]]) with {{ill|Toneri-ō|ja|舎人王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toneri-ō|1=Toneri no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65279725}},<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Emperor Tenmu, 9th year, July 25 entry</ref> and in January of the 1st year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] ([[:en:687|687]]) with {{ill|Abe Goshujin|ja|阿倍御主人|lt=Fuse Goshujin|lt_ja=布勢御主人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Goshujin|1=Abe no Miushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657245}}.<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Empress Jitō, January 1 entry</ref> Since no ministers were appointed during the Tenmu period, the position of Nagon was the highest administrative office. The title “Nagon” is of Chinese origin, as seen in the "Shuntian" of the [[:en:Book of Documents|Shu Jing]], and in the early Sui and Tang dynasties it was the title for the head ([[:en:Palace Attendant|Chamberlain]]) of the [[:en:Menxia Sheng|Monkashō]].
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}} * Mother: Unknown * Children by an unknown consort: ** Son: {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} ** Son: {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kooharu|1=Kose Kooharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162107}}

His [[:en:Kabane|Surname]] is of the type [[:en:Omi (title)|Subject]]. He was the son of the [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}}, and the father of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} and {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kooharu|1=Kose Kooharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162107}}.<ref>'Kōkei Honin': Clause of Kose no Asomi Sakaimaro in Tenpyō 9</ref> His court Rank, as indicated by the {{ill|26‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位二十六階|lt=Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26‐step Court Rank|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209277}}, was that of {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshikinchu|1=Shōkinchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464366}} and [[:en:Chūnagon|Chunagon]], though there are doubts regarding his official positions.
== Analysis == Although many details of his career and life remain unclear, from the obituary of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]<ref>'Shoku Nihongi': Kamiki 1, June 6 entry</ref> the following can be ascertained: # His court Rank, {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshikinchu|1=Shōkinchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464366}}, was the 11th Rank of the 26‐step system established in February of the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]’s reign ([[:en:664|664]]), and although it was a high Rank, by January of the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}’s reign ([[:en:685|685]]) the {{ill|48‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|lt=48‐step Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位四十八階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/48‐step Court Rank|1=history of court rank systems in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395032}} was established. # [[:en:Chūnagon|Chunagon]] was established in April of the 2nd year of [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]] ([[:en:705|705]]) under the title of {{ill|Reigai-kan|ja|令外官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reigai-kan|1=East Asian extra-statutory office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378315}}, a position that did not exist during the Tenji and Tenmu periods. It is presumed that once established during the Tenmu period the character “中” was later inserted into the title “納言”.<ref>{{ill|Kazuo Aoki|ja|青木和夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuo Aoki|1=Kazuo Aoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661670}}: "Reading {{ill|Taku Tanaka|ja|田中卓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taku Tanaka|1=Takashi Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575370}}’s ‘Chunagon (Part 1)’" in ''Shoku Nihongi Kenkyū Ichi – Hachi''</ref> The title “Nagon” appears in instances such as in the 9th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ([[:en:680|680]]) with {{ill|Toneri-ō|ja|舎人王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toneri-ō|1=Toneri no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65279725}},<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Emperor Tenmu, 9th year, July 25 entry</ref> and in January of the 1st year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] ([[:en:687|687]]) with {{ill|Abe Goshujin|ja|阿倍御主人|lt=Fuse Goshujin|lt_ja=布勢御主人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Goshujin|1=Abe no Miushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657245}}.<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Empress Jitō, January 1 entry</ref> Since no ministers were appointed during the Tenmu period, the position of Nagon was the highest administrative office. The title “Nagon” is of Chinese origin, as seen in the "Shuntian" of the [[:en:Book of Documents|Shu Jing]], and in the early Sui and Tang dynasties it was the title for the head ([[:en:Palace Attendant|Chamberlain]]) of the [[:en:Menxia Sheng|Monkashō]].
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}} * Mother: Unknown * Children by an unknown consort: ** Son: {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} ** Son: {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kooharu|1=Kose Kooharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162107}}
