<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢黒麻呂]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q109287613]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q109287613]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Kose no Kuromaro''' (巨勢 黒麻呂) is an aristocrat of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka Period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Kuromaru'''. His [[:en:Kabane|Surname]] is of the type [[:en:Omi (title)|Subject]]. He was the son of the [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}}, and the father of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} and {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kooharu|1=Kose Kooharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162107}}.<ref>'Kōkei Honin': Clause of Kose no Asomi Sakaimaro in Tenpyō 9</ref> His court Rank, as indicated by the {{ill|26‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位二十六階|lt=Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26‐step Court Rank|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209277}}, was that of {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshikinchu|1=Shōkinchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464366}} and [[:en:Chūnagon|Chunagon]], though there are doubts regarding his official positions.
== Analysis == Although many details of his career and life remain unclear, from the obituary of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]<ref>'Shoku Nihongi': Kamiki 1, June 6 entry</ref> the following can be ascertained: # His court Rank, {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshikinchu|1=Shōkinchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464366}}, was the 11th Rank of the 26‐step system established in February of the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]’s reign ([[:en:664|664]]), and although it was a high Rank, by January of the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}’s reign ([[:en:685|685]]) the {{ill|48‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|lt=48‐step Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位四十八階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/48‐step Court Rank|1=history of court rank systems in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395032}} was established. # [[:en:Chūnagon|Chunagon]] was established in April of the 2nd year of [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]] ([[:en:705|705]]) under the title of {{ill|Reigai-kan|ja|令外官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reigai-kan|1=East Asian extra-statutory office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378315}}, a position that did not exist during the Tenji and Tenmu periods. It is presumed that once established during the Tenmu period the character “中” was later inserted into the title “納言”.<ref>{{ill|Kazuo Aoki|ja|青木和夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuo Aoki|1=Kazuo Aoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661670}}: "Reading {{ill|Taku Tanaka|ja|田中卓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taku Tanaka|1=Takashi Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575370}}’s ‘Chunagon (Part 1)’" in ''Shoku Nihongi Kenkyū Ichi – Hachi''</ref> The title “Nagon” appears in instances such as in the 9th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ([[:en:680|680]]) with {{ill|Toneri-ō|ja|舎人王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toneri-ō|1=Toneri no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65279725}},<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Emperor Tenmu, 9th year, July 25 entry</ref> and in January of the 1st year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] ([[:en:687|687]]) with {{ill|Abe Goshujin|ja|阿倍御主人|lt=Fuse Goshujin|lt_ja=布勢御主人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Goshujin|1=Abe no Miushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657245}}.<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Empress Jitō, January 1 entry</ref> Since no ministers were appointed during the Tenmu period, the position of Nagon was the highest administrative office. The title “Nagon” is of Chinese origin, as seen in the "Shuntian" of the [[:en:Book of Documents|Shu Jing]], and in the early Sui and Tang dynasties it was the title for the head ([[:en:Palace Attendant|Chamberlain]]) of the [[:en:Menxia Sheng|Monkashō]].
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}} * Mother: Unknown * Children by an unknown consort: ** Son: {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} ** Son: {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kooharu|1=Kose Kooharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162107}}

His [[:en:Kabane|Surname]] is of the type [[:en:Omi (title)|Subject]]. He was the son of the [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}}, and the father of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} and {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kooharu|1=Kose Kooharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162107}}.<ref>'Kōkei Honin': Clause of Kose no Asomi Sakaimaro in Tenpyō 9</ref> His court Rank, as indicated by the {{ill|26‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位二十六階|lt=Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26‐step Court Rank|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209277}}, was that of {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshikinchu|1=Shōkinchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464366}} and [[:en:Chūnagon|Chunagon]], though there are doubts regarding his official positions.
== Analysis == Although many details of his career and life remain unclear, from the obituary of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]<ref>'Shoku Nihongi': Kamiki 1, June 6 entry</ref> the following can be ascertained: # His court Rank, {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshikinchu|1=Shōkinchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464366}}, was the 11th Rank of the 26‐step system established in February of the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]’s reign ([[:en:664|664]]), and although it was a high Rank, by January of the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}’s reign ([[:en:685|685]]) the {{ill|48‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|lt=48‐step Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位四十八階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/48‐step Court Rank|1=history of court rank systems in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395032}} was established. # [[:en:Chūnagon|Chunagon]] was established in April of the 2nd year of [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]] ([[:en:705|705]]) under the title of {{ill|Reigai-kan|ja|令外官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reigai-kan|1=East Asian extra-statutory office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378315}}, a position that did not exist during the Tenji and Tenmu periods. It is presumed that once established during the Tenmu period the character “中” was later inserted into the title “納言”.<ref>{{ill|Kazuo Aoki|ja|青木和夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuo Aoki|1=Kazuo Aoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661670}}: "Reading {{ill|Taku Tanaka|ja|田中卓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taku Tanaka|1=Takashi Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575370}}’s ‘Chunagon (Part 1)’" in ''Shoku Nihongi Kenkyū Ichi – Hachi''</ref> The title “Nagon” appears in instances such as in the 9th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ([[:en:680|680]]) with {{ill|Toneri-ō|ja|舎人王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toneri-ō|1=Toneri no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65279725}},<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Emperor Tenmu, 9th year, July 25 entry</ref> and in January of the 1st year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] ([[:en:687|687]]) with {{ill|Abe Goshujin|ja|阿倍御主人|lt=Fuse Goshujin|lt_ja=布勢御主人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Goshujin|1=Abe no Miushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657245}}.<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Empress Jitō, January 1 entry</ref> Since no ministers were appointed during the Tenmu period, the position of Nagon was the highest administrative office. The title “Nagon” is of Chinese origin, as seen in the "Shuntian" of the [[:en:Book of Documents|Shu Jing]], and in the early Sui and Tang dynasties it was the title for the head ([[:en:Palace Attendant|Chamberlain]]) of the [[:en:Menxia Sheng|Monkashō]].
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}} * Mother: Unknown * Children by an unknown consort: ** Son: {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} ** Son: {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kooharu|1=Kose Kooharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162107}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢黒麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢黒麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢黒麻呂]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q109287613]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q109287613]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Kose no Kuromaro''' (巨勢 黒麻呂) is an aristocrat of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka Period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Kuromaru'''. His [[:en:Kabane|Surname]] is of the type [[:en:Omi (title)|Subject]]. He was the son of the [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}}, and the father of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} and {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kooharu|1=Kose Kooharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162107}}.<ref>'Kōkei Honin': Clause of Kose no Asomi Sakaimaro in Tenpyō 9</ref> His court Rank, as indicated by the {{ill|26‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位二十六階|lt=Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26‐step Court Rank|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209277}}, was that of {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshikinchu|1=Shōkinchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464366}} and [[:en:Chūnagon|Chunagon]], though there are doubts regarding his official positions.
== Analysis == Although many details of his career and life remain unclear, from the obituary of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]<ref>'Shoku Nihongi': Kamiki 1, June 6 entry</ref> the following can be ascertained: # His court Rank, {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshikinchu|1=Shōkinchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464366}}, was the 11th Rank of the 26‐step system established in February of the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]’s reign ([[:en:664|664]]), and although it was a high Rank, by January of the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}’s reign ([[:en:685|685]]) the {{ill|48‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|lt=48‐step Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位四十八階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/48‐step Court Rank|1=history of court rank systems in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395032}} was established. # [[:en:Chūnagon|Chunagon]] was established in April of the 2nd year of [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]] ([[:en:705|705]]) under the title of {{ill|Reigai-kan|ja|令外官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reigai-kan|1=East Asian extra-statutory office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378315}}, a position that did not exist during the Tenji and Tenmu periods. It is presumed that once established during the Tenmu period the character “中” was later inserted into the title “納言”.<ref>{{ill|Kazuo Aoki|ja|青木和夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuo Aoki|1=Kazuo Aoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661670}}: "Reading {{ill|Taku Tanaka|ja|田中卓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taku Tanaka|1=Takashi Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575370}}’s ‘Chunagon (Part 1)’" in ''Shoku Nihongi Kenkyū Ichi – Hachi''</ref> The title “Nagon” appears in instances such as in the 9th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ([[:en:680|680]]) with {{ill|Toneri-ō|ja|舎人王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toneri-ō|1=Toneri no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65279725}},<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Emperor Tenmu, 9th year, July 25 entry</ref> and in January of the 1st year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] ([[:en:687|687]]) with {{ill|Abe Goshujin|ja|阿倍御主人|lt=Fuse Goshujin|lt_ja=布勢御主人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Goshujin|1=Abe no Miushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657245}}.<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Empress Jitō, January 1 entry</ref> Since no ministers were appointed during the Tenmu period, the position of Nagon was the highest administrative office. The title “Nagon” is of Chinese origin, as seen in the "Shuntian" of the [[:en:Book of Documents|Shu Jing]], and in the early Sui and Tang dynasties it was the title for the head ([[:en:Palace Attendant|Chamberlain]]) of the [[:en:Menxia Sheng|Monkashō]].
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}} * Mother: Unknown * Children by an unknown consort: ** Son: {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} ** Son: {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kooharu|1=Kose Kooharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162107}}

His [[:en:Kabane|Surname]] is of the type [[:en:Omi (title)|Subject]]. He was the son of the [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}}, and the father of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} and {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kooharu|1=Kose Kooharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162107}}.<ref>'Kōkei Honin': Clause of Kose no Asomi Sakaimaro in Tenpyō 9</ref> His court Rank, as indicated by the {{ill|26‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位二十六階|lt=Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26‐step Court Rank|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209277}}, was that of {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshikinchu|1=Shōkinchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464366}} and [[:en:Chūnagon|Chunagon]], though there are doubts regarding his official positions.
== Analysis == Although many details of his career and life remain unclear, from the obituary of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]<ref>'Shoku Nihongi': Kamiki 1, June 6 entry</ref> the following can be ascertained: # His court Rank, {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshikinchu|1=Shōkinchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464366}}, was the 11th Rank of the 26‐step system established in February of the 3rd year of [[:en:Emperor Tenji|Emperor Tenji]]’s reign ([[:en:664|664]]), and although it was a high Rank, by January of the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}’s reign ([[:en:685|685]]) the {{ill|48‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|lt=48‐step Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位四十八階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/48‐step Court Rank|1=history of court rank systems in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395032}} was established. # [[:en:Chūnagon|Chunagon]] was established in April of the 2nd year of [[:en:Keiun|Keiun]] ([[:en:705|705]]) under the title of {{ill|Reigai-kan|ja|令外官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reigai-kan|1=East Asian extra-statutory office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378315}}, a position that did not exist during the Tenji and Tenmu periods. It is presumed that once established during the Tenmu period the character “中” was later inserted into the title “納言”.<ref>{{ill|Kazuo Aoki|ja|青木和夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuo Aoki|1=Kazuo Aoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661670}}: "Reading {{ill|Taku Tanaka|ja|田中卓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taku Tanaka|1=Takashi Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575370}}’s ‘Chunagon (Part 1)’" in ''Shoku Nihongi Kenkyū Ichi – Hachi''</ref> The title “Nagon” appears in instances such as in the 9th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ([[:en:680|680]]) with {{ill|Toneri-ō|ja|舎人王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toneri-ō|1=Toneri no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65279725}},<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Emperor Tenmu, 9th year, July 25 entry</ref> and in January of the 1st year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] ([[:en:687|687]]) with {{ill|Abe Goshujin|ja|阿倍御主人|lt=Fuse Goshujin|lt_ja=布勢御主人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Goshujin|1=Abe no Miushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657245}}.<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Empress Jitō, January 1 entry</ref> Since no ministers were appointed during the Tenmu period, the position of Nagon was the highest administrative office. The title “Nagon” is of Chinese origin, as seen in the "Shuntian" of the [[:en:Book of Documents|Shu Jing]], and in the early Sui and Tang dynasties it was the title for the head ([[:en:Palace Attendant|Chamberlain]]) of the [[:en:Menxia Sheng|Monkashō]].
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}} * Mother: Unknown * Children by an unknown consort: ** Son: {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} ** Son: {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kooharu|1=Kose Kooharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162107}}
== References ==

'''Kose no Kuromaro''' (巨勢 黒麻呂) is an aristocrat of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka Period]]. His name is also recorded as '''Kuromaru'''. His [[:en:Kabane|Surname]] is of the type [[:en:Omi (title)|Subject]]. He was the son of the [[:en:Minister of the Left|Minister of the Left]] {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}}, and the father of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} and {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kooharu|1=Kose Kooharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162107}}.<ref>'Kōkei Honin': Clause of Kose no Asomi Sakaimaro in Tenpyō 9</ref> His court Rank, as indicated by the {{ill|26‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位二十六階|lt=Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26‐step Court Rank|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209277}}, was that of {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshikinchu|1=Shōkinchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464366}} and [[:en:Chūnagon|Chunagon]], though there are doubts regarding his official positions.

It is presumed that once established during the Tenmu period the character “中” was later inserted into the title “納言”.<ref>{{ill|Kazuo Aoki|ja|青木和夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuo Aoki|1=Kazuo Aoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661670}}: "Reading {{ill|Taku Tanaka|ja|田中卓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taku Tanaka|1=Takashi Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575370}}’s ‘Chunagon (Part 1)’" in ''Shoku Nihongi Kenkyū Ichi – Hachi''</ref> The title “Nagon” appears in instances such as in the 9th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ([[:en:680|680]]) with {{ill|Toneri-ō|ja|舎人王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toneri-ō|1=Toneri no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65279725}},<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Emperor Tenmu, 9th year, July 25 entry</ref> and in January of the 1st year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Empress Jitō]] ([[:en:687|687]]) with {{ill|Abe Goshujin|ja|阿倍御主人|lt=Fuse Goshujin|lt_ja=布勢御主人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Goshujin|1=Abe no Miushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657245}}.<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Empress Jitō, January 1 entry</ref> Since no ministers were appointed during the Tenmu period, the position of Nagon was the highest administrative office. The title “Nagon” is of Chinese origin, as seen in the "Shuntian" of the [[:en:Book of Documents|Shu Jing]], and in the early Sui and Tang dynasties it was the title for the head ([[:en:Palace Attendant|Chamberlain]]) of the [[:en:Menxia Sheng|Monkashō]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢黒麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢黒麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢黒麻呂]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q109287613]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q109287613]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Kose no Kuromaro''' (巨勢 黒麻呂) is an aristocrat of the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka Period}}. His name is also recorded as '''Kuromaru'''. His {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Surname}} is of the type {{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|WD=Q2574718|lt=Subject}}. He was the son of the {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left}} {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}}, and the father of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} and {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kooharu|1=Kose Kooharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162107}}.<ref>'Kōkei Honin': Clause of Kose no Asomi Sakaimaro in Tenpyō 9</ref> His court Rank, as indicated by the {{ill|26‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位二十六階|lt=Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26‐step Court Rank|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209277}}, was that of {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshikinchu|1=Shōkinchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464366}} and {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chunagon}}, though there are doubts regarding his official positions.
== Analysis == Although many details of his career and life remain unclear, from the obituary of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi}}<ref>'Shoku Nihongi': Kamiki 1, June 6 entry</ref> the following can be ascertained: # His court Rank, {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshikinchu|1=Shōkinchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464366}}, was the 11th Rank of the 26‐step system established in February of the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}}’s reign ({{ill|664|en|664|WD=Q30621|lt=664}}), and although it was a high Rank, by January of the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}’s reign ({{ill|685|en|685|WD=Q33480|lt=685}}) the {{ill|48‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|lt=48‐step Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位四十八階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/48‐step Court Rank|1=history of court rank systems in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395032}} was established. # {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chunagon}} was established in April of the 2nd year of {{ill|Keiun|en|Keiun|WD=Q428753|lt=Keiun}} ({{ill|705|en|705|WD=Q23504|lt=705}}) under the title of {{ill|Reigai-kan|ja|令外官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reigai-kan|1=East Asian extra-statutory office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378315}}, a position that did not exist during the Tenji and Tenmu periods. It is presumed that once established during the Tenmu period the character “中” was later inserted into the title “納言”.<ref>{{ill|Kazuo Aoki|ja|青木和夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuo Aoki|1=Kazuo Aoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661670}}: "Reading {{ill|Taku Tanaka|ja|田中卓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taku Tanaka|1=Takashi Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575370}}’s ‘Chunagon (Part 1)’" in ''Shoku Nihongi Kenkyū Ichi – Hachi''</ref> The title “Nagon” appears in instances such as in the 9th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ({{ill|680|en|680|WD=Q23453|lt=680}}) with {{ill|Toneri-ō|ja|舎人王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toneri-ō|1=Toneri no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65279725}},<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Emperor Tenmu, 9th year, July 25 entry</ref> and in January of the 1st year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō}} ({{ill|687|en|687|WD=Q30303|lt=687}}) with {{ill|Abe Goshujin|ja|阿倍御主人|lt=Fuse Goshujin|lt_ja=布勢御主人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Goshujin|1=Abe no Miushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657245}}.<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Empress Jitō, January 1 entry</ref> Since no ministers were appointed during the Tenmu period, the position of Nagon was the highest administrative office. The title “Nagon” is of Chinese origin, as seen in the "Shuntian" of the {{ill|Book of Documents|en|Book of Documents|WD=Q875313|lt=Shu Jing}}, and in the early Sui and Tang dynasties it was the title for the head ({{ill|Palace Attendant|en|Palace Attendant|WD=Q5368120|lt=Chamberlain}}) of the {{ill|Menxia Sheng|en|Menxia Sheng|WD=Q10839649|lt=Monkashō}}.
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}} * Mother: Unknown * Children by an unknown consort: ** Son: {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} ** Son: {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kooharu|1=Kose Kooharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162107}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * 『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}』 (Volume 5), {{ill|Iwanami Bunko|ja|岩波文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanami Bunko|1=Iwanami Bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11475322}}, 1995 * 『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}』 Complete Modern Translation (Lower Volume), {{ill|Kodansha Academic Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodansha Academic Bunko|1=Kōdansha gakujutsu bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632220}}, translated by {{ill|Mou Ujiyatani|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mou Ujiyatani|1=Tsutomu Ujitani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449517}}, 1988 * 『{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi}}』 Vol. 2, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|en|Iwanami Shoten|WD=Q1331427|lt=Iwanami Shoten}}, 1990 * {{ill|Mou Ujiyatani|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mou Ujiyatani|1=Tsutomu Ujitani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449517}} 『Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)』, {{ill|Kodansha Academic Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodansha Academic Bunko|1=Kōdansha gakujutsu bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632220}}, 1992

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢黒麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢黒麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢黒麻呂]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q109287613]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q109287613]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Kose no Kuromaro''' (巨勢 黒麻呂) is an aristocrat of the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka Period}}. His name is also recorded as '''Kuromaru'''. His {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Surname}} is of the type {{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|WD=Q2574718|lt=Subject}}. He was the son of the {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left}} {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}}, and the father of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} and {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kooharu|1=Kose Kooharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162107}}.<ref>'Kōkei Honin': Clause of Kose no Asomi Sakaimaro in Tenpyō 9</ref> His court Rank, as indicated by the {{ill|26‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位二十六階|lt=Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26‐step Court Rank|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209277}}, was that of {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshikinchu|1=Shōkinchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464366}} and {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chunagon}}, though there are doubts regarding his official positions.
== Analysis == Although many details of his career and life remain unclear, from the obituary of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi}}<ref>'Shoku Nihongi': Kamiki 1, June 6 entry</ref> the following can be ascertained: # His court Rank, {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshikinchu|1=Shōkinchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464366}}, was the 11th Rank of the 26‐step system established in February of the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}}’s reign ({{ill|664|en|664|WD=Q30621|lt=664}}), and although it was a high Rank, by January of the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}’s reign ({{ill|685|en|685|WD=Q33480|lt=685}}) the {{ill|48‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|lt=48‐step Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位四十八階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/48‐step Court Rank|1=history of court rank systems in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395032}} was established. # {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chunagon}} was established in April of the 2nd year of {{ill|Keiun|en|Keiun|WD=Q428753|lt=Keiun}} ({{ill|705|en|705|WD=Q23504|lt=705}}) under the title of {{ill|Reigai-kan|ja|令外官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reigai-kan|1=East Asian extra-statutory office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378315}}, a position that did not exist during the Tenji and Tenmu periods. It is presumed that once established during the Tenmu period the character “中” was later inserted into the title “納言”.<ref>{{ill|Kazuo Aoki|ja|青木和夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuo Aoki|1=Kazuo Aoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661670}}: "Reading {{ill|Taku Tanaka|ja|田中卓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taku Tanaka|1=Takashi Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575370}}’s ‘Chunagon (Part 1)’" in ''Shoku Nihongi Kenkyū Ichi – Hachi''</ref> The title “Nagon” appears in instances such as in the 9th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ({{ill|680|en|680|WD=Q23453|lt=680}}) with {{ill|Toneri-ō|ja|舎人王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toneri-ō|1=Toneri no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65279725}},<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Emperor Tenmu, 9th year, July 25 entry</ref> and in January of the 1st year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō}} ({{ill|687|en|687|WD=Q30303|lt=687}}) with {{ill|Abe Goshujin|ja|阿倍御主人|lt=Fuse Goshujin|lt_ja=布勢御主人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Goshujin|1=Abe no Miushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657245}}.<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Empress Jitō, January 1 entry</ref> Since no ministers were appointed during the Tenmu period, the position of Nagon was the highest administrative office. The title “Nagon” is of Chinese origin, as seen in the "Shuntian" of the {{ill|Book of Documents|en|Book of Documents|WD=Q875313|lt=Shu Jing}}, and in the early Sui and Tang dynasties it was the title for the head ({{ill|Palace Attendant|en|Palace Attendant|WD=Q5368120|lt=Chamberlain}}) of the {{ill|Menxia Sheng|en|Menxia Sheng|WD=Q10839649|lt=Monkashō}}.
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}} * Mother: Unknown * Children by an unknown consort: ** Son: {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} ** Son: {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kooharu|1=Kose Kooharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162107}}
== References ==

== Bibliography == * 『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}』 (Volume 5), {{ill|Iwanami Bunko|ja|岩波文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanami Bunko|1=Iwanami Bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11475322}}, 1995 * 『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}』 Complete Modern Translation (Lower Volume), {{ill|Kodansha Academic Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodansha Academic Bunko|1=Kōdansha gakujutsu bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632220}}, translated by {{ill|Mou Ujiyatani|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mou Ujiyatani|1=Tsutomu Ujitani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449517}}, 1988 * 『{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi}}』 Vol. 2, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|en|Iwanami Shoten|WD=Q1331427|lt=Iwanami Shoten}}, 1990 * {{ill|Mou Ujiyatani|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mou Ujiyatani|1=Tsutomu Ujitani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449517}} 『Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)』, {{ill|Kodansha Academic Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodansha Academic Bunko|1=Kōdansha gakujutsu bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632220}}, 1992

It is presumed that once established during the Tenmu period the character “中” was later inserted into the title “納言”.<ref>{{ill|Kazuo Aoki|ja|青木和夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuo Aoki|1=Kazuo Aoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661670}}: "Reading {{ill|Taku Tanaka|ja|田中卓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taku Tanaka|1=Takashi Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575370}}’s ‘Chunagon (Part 1)’" in ''Shoku Nihongi Kenkyū Ichi – Hachi''</ref> The title “Nagon” appears in instances such as in the 9th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ({{ill|680|en|680|WD=Q23453|lt=680}}) with {{ill|Toneri-ō|ja|舎人王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toneri-ō|1=Toneri no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65279725}},<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Emperor Tenmu, 9th year, July 25 entry</ref> and in January of the 1st year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō}} ({{ill|687|en|687|WD=Q30303|lt=687}}) with {{ill|Abe Goshujin|ja|阿倍御主人|lt=Fuse Goshujin|lt_ja=布勢御主人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Goshujin|1=Abe no Miushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657245}}.<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Empress Jitō, January 1 entry</ref> Since no ministers were appointed during the Tenmu period, the position of Nagon was the highest administrative office. The title “Nagon” is of Chinese origin, as seen in the "Shuntian" of the {{ill|Book of Documents|en|Book of Documents|WD=Q875313|lt=Shu Jing}}, and in the early Sui and Tang dynasties it was the title for the head ({{ill|Palace Attendant|en|Palace Attendant|WD=Q5368120|lt=Chamberlain}}) of the {{ill|Menxia Sheng|en|Menxia Sheng|WD=Q10839649|lt=Monkashō}}.
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}} * Mother: Unknown * Children by an unknown consort: ** Son: {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} ** Son: {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kooharu|1=Kose Kooharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162107}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * 『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}』 (Volume 5), {{ill|Iwanami Bunko|ja|岩波文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanami Bunko|1=Iwanami Bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11475322}}, 1995 * 『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}』 Complete Modern Translation (Lower Volume), {{ill|Kodansha Academic Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodansha Academic Bunko|1=Kōdansha gakujutsu bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632220}}, translated by {{ill|Mou Ujiyatani|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mou Ujiyatani|1=Tsutomu Ujitani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449517}}, 1988 * 『{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi}}』 Vol. 2, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|en|Iwanami Shoten|WD=Q1331427|lt=Iwanami Shoten}}, 1990 * {{ill|Mou Ujiyatani|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mou Ujiyatani|1=Tsutomu Ujitani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449517}} 『Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)』, {{ill|Kodansha Academic Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodansha Academic Bunko|1=Kōdansha gakujutsu bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632220}}, 1992

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢黒麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢黒麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢黒麻呂]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}
'''Kose no Kuromaro''' (巨勢 黒麻呂) is an aristocrat of the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka Period}}. His name is also recorded as '''Kuromaru'''. His {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Surname}} is of the type {{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|WD=Q2574718|lt=Subject}}. He was the son of the {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left}} {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}}, and the father of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} and {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kooharu|1=Kose Kooharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162107}}.<ref>'Kōkei Honin': Clause of Kose no Asomi Sakaimaro in Tenpyō 9</ref> His court Rank, as indicated by the {{ill|26‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位二十六階|lt=Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26‐step Court Rank|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209277}}, was that of {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshikinchu|1=Shōkinchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464366}} and {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chunagon}}, though there are doubts regarding his official positions.
== Analysis == Although many details of his career and life remain unclear, from the obituary of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi}}<ref>'Shoku Nihongi': Kamiki 1, June 6 entry</ref> the following can be ascertained: # His court Rank, {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshikinchu|1=Shōkinchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464366}}, was the 11th Rank of the 26‐step system established in February of the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji}}’s reign ({{ill|664|en|664|WD=Q30621|lt=664}}), and although it was a high Rank, by January of the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}’s reign ({{ill|685|en|685|WD=Q33480|lt=685}}) the {{ill|48‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|lt=48‐step Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位四十八階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/48‐step Court Rank|1=history of court rank systems in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395032}} was established. # {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chunagon}} was established in April of the 2nd year of {{ill|Keiun|en|Keiun|WD=Q428753|lt=Keiun}} ({{ill|705|en|705|WD=Q23504|lt=705}}) under the title of {{ill|Reigai-kan|ja|令外官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reigai-kan|1=East Asian extra-statutory office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378315}}, a position that did not exist during the Tenji and Tenmu periods. It is presumed that once established during the Tenmu period the character “中” was later inserted into the title “納言”.<ref>{{ill|Kazuo Aoki|ja|青木和夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuo Aoki|1=Kazuo Aoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661670}}: "Reading {{ill|Taku Tanaka|ja|田中卓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taku Tanaka|1=Takashi Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575370}}’s ‘Chunagon (Part 1)’" in ''Shoku Nihongi Kenkyū Ichi – Hachi''</ref> The title “Nagon” appears in instances such as in the 9th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ({{ill|680|en|680|WD=Q23453|lt=680}}) with {{ill|Toneri-ō|ja|舎人王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toneri-ō|1=Toneri no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65279725}},<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Emperor Tenmu, 9th year, July 25 entry</ref> and in January of the 1st year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō}} ({{ill|687|en|687|WD=Q30303|lt=687}}) with {{ill|Abe Goshujin|ja|阿倍御主人|lt=Fuse Goshujin|lt_ja=布勢御主人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Goshujin|1=Abe no Miushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657245}}.<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Empress Jitō, January 1 entry</ref> Since no ministers were appointed during the Tenmu period, the position of Nagon was the highest administrative office. The title “Nagon” is of Chinese origin, as seen in the "Shuntian" of the {{ill|Book of Documents|en|Book of Documents|WD=Q875313|lt=Shu Jing}}, and in the early Sui and Tang dynasties it was the title for the head ({{ill|Palace Attendant|en|Palace Attendant|WD=Q5368120|lt=Chamberlain}}) of the {{ill|Menxia Sheng|en|Menxia Sheng|WD=Q10839649|lt=Monkashō}}.
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}} * Mother: Unknown * Children by an unknown consort: ** Son: {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} ** Son: {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kooharu|1=Kose Kooharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162107}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * 『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}』 (Volume 5), {{ill|Iwanami Bunko|ja|岩波文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanami Bunko|1=Iwanami Bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11475322}}, 1995 * 『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}』 Complete Modern Translation (Lower Volume), {{ill|Kodansha Academic Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodansha Academic Bunko|1=Kōdansha gakujutsu bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632220}}, translated by {{ill|Mou Ujiyatani|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mou Ujiyatani|1=Tsutomu Ujitani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449517}}, 1988 * 『{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi}}』 Vol. 2, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|en|Iwanami Shoten|WD=Q1331427|lt=Iwanami Shoten}}, 1990 * {{ill|Mou Ujiyatani|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mou Ujiyatani|1=Tsutomu Ujitani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449517}} 『Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)』, {{ill|Kodansha Academic Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodansha Academic Bunko|1=Kōdansha gakujutsu bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632220}}, 1992

'''Kose no Kuromaro''' (巨勢 黒麻呂) is an aristocrat of the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka Period}}. His name is also recorded as '''Kuromaru'''. His {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Surname}} is of the type {{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|WD=Q2574718|lt=Subject}}. He was the son of the {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left}} {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}}, and the father of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose Oharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} and {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kooharu|1=Kose Kooharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162107}}.<ref>'Kōkei Honin': Clause of Kose no Asomi Sakaimaro in Tenpyō 9</ref> His court Rank, as indicated by the {{ill|26‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位二十六階|lt=Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26‐step Court Rank|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209277}}, was that of {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshikinchu|1=Shōkinchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464366}} and {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chunagon}}, though there are doubts regarding his official positions.

== Bibliography == * 『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}』 (Volume 5), {{ill|Iwanami Bunko|ja|岩波文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanami Bunko|1=Iwanami Bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11475322}}, 1995 * 『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}』 Complete Modern Translation (Lower Volume), {{ill|Kodansha Academic Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodansha Academic Bunko|1=Kōdansha gakujutsu bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632220}}, translated by {{ill|Mou Ujiyatani|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mou Ujiyatani|1=Tsutomu Ujitani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449517}}, 1988 * 『{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi}}』 Vol. 2, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|en|Iwanami Shoten|WD=Q1331427|lt=Iwanami Shoten}}, 1990 * {{ill|Mou Ujiyatani|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mou Ujiyatani|1=Tsutomu Ujitani|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449517}} 『Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)』, {{ill|Kodansha Academic Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodansha Academic Bunko|1=Kōdansha gakujutsu bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632220}}, 1992

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢黒麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢黒麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢黒麻呂]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}}}
'''Kose no Kuromaro''' (巨勢 黒麻呂) is an aristocrat of the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka Period|1=Asuka period}}. His name is also recorded as '''Kuromaru'''. His {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Surname|1=Kabane}} is of the type {{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|WD=Q2574718|lt=Subject|1=Omi (title)}}. He was the son of the {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left|1=Minister of the Left}} {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}}, and the father of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose no Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} and {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kooharu|1=Kooharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162107}}.<ref>'Kōkei Honin': Clause of Kose no Asomi Sakaimaro in Tenpyō 9</ref> His court Rank, as indicated by the {{ill|26‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位二十六階|lt=Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26‐step Court Rank|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209277}}, was that of {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshikinchu|1=Shōkinchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464366}} and {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chunagon|1=Chūnagon}}, though there are doubts regarding his official positions.
== Analysis == Although many details of his career and life remain unclear, from the obituary of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose no Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}}<ref>'Shoku Nihongi': Kamiki 1, June 6 entry</ref> the following can be ascertained: # His court Rank, {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshikinchu|1=Shōkinchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464366}}, was the 11th Rank of the 26‐step system established in February of the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}}’s reign ({{ill|664|en|664|WD=Q30621|lt=664|1=664}}), and although it was a high Rank, by January of the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}’s reign ({{ill|685|en|685|WD=Q33480|lt=685|1=685}}) the {{ill|48‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|lt=48‐step Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位四十八階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/48‐step Court Rank|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395032}} was established. # {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chunagon|1=Chūnagon}} was established in April of the 2nd year of {{ill|Keiun|en|Keiun|WD=Q428753|lt=Keiun|1=Keiun}} ({{ill|705|en|705|WD=Q23504|lt=705|1=705}}) under the title of {{ill|Reigai-kan|ja|令外官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reigai-kan|1=East Asian extra-statutory office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378315}}, a position that did not exist during the Tenji and Tenmu periods. It is presumed that once established during the Tenmu period the character “中” was later inserted into the title “納言”.<ref>{{ill|Kazuo Aoki|ja|青木和夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuo Aoki|1=Kazuo Aoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661670}}: "Reading {{ill|Taku Tanaka|ja|田中卓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taku Tanaka|1=Takashi Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575370}}’s ‘Chunagon (Part 1)’" in ''Shoku Nihongi Kenkyū Ichi – Hachi''</ref> The title “Nagon” appears in instances such as in the 9th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ({{ill|680|en|680|WD=Q23453|lt=680|1=680}}) with {{ill|Toneri-ō|ja|舎人王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toneri-ō|1=Toneri no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65279725}},<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Emperor Tenmu, 9th year, July 25 entry</ref> and in January of the 1st year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō|1=Empress Jitō}} ({{ill|687|en|687|WD=Q30303|lt=687|1=687}}) with {{ill|Abe Goshujin|ja|阿倍御主人|lt=Fuse Goshujin|lt_ja=布勢御主人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Goshujin|1=Abe no Miushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657245}}.<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Empress Jitō, January 1 entry</ref> Since no ministers were appointed during the Tenmu period, the position of Nagon was the highest administrative office. The title “Nagon” is of Chinese origin, as seen in the "Shuntian" of the {{ill|Book of Documents|en|Book of Documents|WD=Q875313|lt=Shu Jing|1=Book of Documents}}, and in the early Sui and Tang dynasties it was the title for the head ({{ill|Palace Attendant|en|Palace Attendant|WD=Q5368120|lt=Chamberlain|1=Palace Attendant}}) of the {{ill|Menxia Sheng|en|Menxia Sheng|WD=Q10839649|lt=Monkashō|1=Menxia Sheng}}.
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}} * Mother: Unknown * Children by an unknown consort: ** Son: {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose no Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} ** Son: {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kooharu|1=Kooharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162107}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * 『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}』 (Volume 5), {{ill|Iwanami Bunko|ja|岩波文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanami Bunko|1=Iwanami Bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11475322}}, 1995 * 『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}』 Complete Modern Translation (Lower Volume), {{ill|Kodansha Academic Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodansha Academic Bunko|1=Kōdansha gakujutsu bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632220}}, translated by {{ill|Mou Ujiyatani|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mou Ujiyatani|1=Ujitani Mitsuru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449517}}, 1988 * 『{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}}』 Vol. 2, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|en|Iwanami Shoten|WD=Q1331427|lt=Iwanami Shoten|1=Iwanami Shoten}}, 1990 * {{ill|Mou Ujiyatani|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mou Ujiyatani|1=Ujitani Mitsuru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449517}} 『Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)』, {{ill|Kodansha Academic Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodansha Academic Bunko|1=Kōdansha gakujutsu bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632220}}, 1992

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢黒麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢黒麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢黒麻呂]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}}}
'''Kose no Kuromaro''' (巨勢 黒麻呂) is an aristocrat of the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka Period|1=Asuka period}}. His name is also recorded as '''Kuromaru'''. His {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Surname|1=Kabane}} is of the type {{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|WD=Q2574718|lt=Subject|1=Omi (title)}}. He was the son of the {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left|1=Minister of the Left}} {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}}, and the father of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose no Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} and {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kooharu|1=Kooharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162107}}.<ref>'Kōkei Honin': Clause of Kose no Asomi Sakaimaro in Tenpyō 9</ref> His court Rank, as indicated by the {{ill|26‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位二十六階|lt=Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26‐step Court Rank|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209277}}, was that of {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshikinchu|1=Shōkinchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464366}} and {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chunagon|1=Chūnagon}}, though there are doubts regarding his official positions.
== Analysis == Although many details of his career and life remain unclear, from the obituary of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose no Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}}<ref>'Shoku Nihongi': Kamiki 1, June 6 entry</ref> the following can be ascertained: # His court Rank, {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshikinchu|1=Shōkinchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464366}}, was the 11th Rank of the 26‐step system established in February of the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}}’s reign ({{ill|664|en|664|WD=Q30621|lt=664|1=664}}), and although it was a high Rank, by January of the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}’s reign ({{ill|685|en|685|WD=Q33480|lt=685|1=685}}) the {{ill|48‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|lt=48‐step Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位四十八階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/48‐step Court Rank|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395032}} was established. # {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chunagon|1=Chūnagon}} was established in April of the 2nd year of {{ill|Keiun|en|Keiun|WD=Q428753|lt=Keiun|1=Keiun}} ({{ill|705|en|705|WD=Q23504|lt=705|1=705}}) under the title of {{ill|Reigai-kan|ja|令外官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reigai-kan|1=East Asian extra-statutory office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378315}}, a position that did not exist during the Tenji and Tenmu periods. It is presumed that once established during the Tenmu period the character “中” was later inserted into the title “納言”.<ref>{{ill|Kazuo Aoki|ja|青木和夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuo Aoki|1=Kazuo Aoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661670}}: "Reading {{ill|Taku Tanaka|ja|田中卓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taku Tanaka|1=Takashi Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575370}}’s ‘Chunagon (Part 1)’" in ''Shoku Nihongi Kenkyū Ichi – Hachi''</ref> The title “Nagon” appears in instances such as in the 9th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ({{ill|680|en|680|WD=Q23453|lt=680|1=680}}) with {{ill|Toneri-ō|ja|舎人王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toneri-ō|1=Toneri no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65279725}},<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Emperor Tenmu, 9th year, July 25 entry</ref> and in January of the 1st year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō|1=Empress Jitō}} ({{ill|687|en|687|WD=Q30303|lt=687|1=687}}) with {{ill|Abe Goshujin|ja|阿倍御主人|lt=Fuse Goshujin|lt_ja=布勢御主人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Goshujin|1=Abe no Miushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657245}}.<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Empress Jitō, January 1 entry</ref> Since no ministers were appointed during the Tenmu period, the position of Nagon was the highest administrative office. The title “Nagon” is of Chinese origin, as seen in the "Shuntian" of the {{ill|Book of Documents|en|Book of Documents|WD=Q875313|lt=Shu Jing|1=Book of Documents}}, and in the early Sui and Tang dynasties it was the title for the head ({{ill|Palace Attendant|en|Palace Attendant|WD=Q5368120|lt=Chamberlain|1=Palace Attendant}}) of the {{ill|Menxia Sheng|en|Menxia Sheng|WD=Q10839649|lt=Monkashō|1=Menxia Sheng}}.
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}} * Mother: Unknown * Children by an unknown consort: ** Son: {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose no Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} ** Son: {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kooharu|1=Kooharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162107}}
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * 『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}』 (Volume 5), {{ill|Iwanami Bunko|ja|岩波文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanami Bunko|1=Iwanami Bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11475322}}, 1995 * 『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}』 Complete Modern Translation (Lower Volume), {{ill|Kodansha Academic Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodansha Academic Bunko|1=Kōdansha gakujutsu bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632220}}, translated by {{ill|Mou Ujiyatani|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mou Ujiyatani|1=Ujitani Mitsuru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449517}}, 1988 * 『{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}}』 Vol. 2, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 13, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|en|Iwanami Shoten|WD=Q1331427|lt=Iwanami Shoten|1=Iwanami Shoten}}, 1990 * {{ill|Mou Ujiyatani|ja|宇治谷孟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mou Ujiyatani|1=Ujitani Mitsuru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449517}} 『Shoku Nihongi (Volume 1)』, {{ill|Kodansha Academic Bunko|ja|講談社学術文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodansha Academic Bunko|1=Kōdansha gakujutsu bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632220}}, 1992

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢黒麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢黒麻呂]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢黒麻呂]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}}}
'''Kose no Kuromaro''' (巨勢 黒麻呂) is an aristocrat of the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka Period|1=Asuka period}}. His name is also recorded as '''Kuromaru'''. His {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Surname|1=Kabane}} is of the type {{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|WD=Q2574718|lt=Subject|1=Omi (title)}}. He was the son of the {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|WD=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left|1=Minister of the Left}} {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}}, and the father of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose no Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} and {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kooharu|1=Kooharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162107}}.<ref>'Kōkei Honin': Clause of Kose no Asomi Sakaimaro in Tenpyō 9</ref> His court Rank, as indicated by the {{ill|26‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位二十六階|lt=Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26‐step Court Rank|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17209277}}, was that of {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshikinchu|1=Shōkinchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464366}} and {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chunagon|1=Chūnagon}}, though there are doubts regarding his official positions.
== Analysis == Although many details of his career and life remain unclear, from the obituary of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose no Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}}<ref>'Shoku Nihongi': Kamiki 1, June 6 entry</ref> the following can be ascertained: # His court Rank, {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshikinchu|1=Shōkinchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11464366}}, was the 11th Rank of the 26‐step system established in February of the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|WD=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}}’s reign ({{ill|664|en|664|WD=Q30621|lt=664|1=664}}), and although it was a high Rank, by January of the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}’s reign ({{ill|685|en|685|WD=Q33480|lt=685|1=685}}) the {{ill|48‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|lt=48‐step Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位四十八階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/48‐step Court Rank|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11395032}} was established. # {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|WD=Q1185382|lt=Chunagon|1=Chūnagon}} was established in April of the 2nd year of {{ill|Keiun|en|Keiun|WD=Q428753|lt=Keiun|1=Keiun}} ({{ill|705|en|705|WD=Q23504|lt=705|1=705}}) under the title of {{ill|Reigai-kan|ja|令外官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reigai-kan|1=East Asian extra-statutory office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378315}}, a position that did not exist during the Tenji and Tenmu periods. It is presumed that once established during the Tenmu period the character “中” was later inserted into the title “納言”.<ref>{{ill|Kazuo Aoki|ja|青木和夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuo Aoki|1=Kazuo Aoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661670}}: "Reading {{ill|Taku Tanaka|ja|田中卓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taku Tanaka|1=Takashi Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11575370}}’s ‘Chunagon (Part 1)’" in ''Shoku Nihongi Kenkyū Ichi – Hachi''</ref> The title “Nagon” appears in instances such as in the 9th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ({{ill|680|en|680|WD=Q23453|lt=680|1=680}}) with {{ill|Toneri-ō|ja|舎人王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toneri-ō|1=Toneri no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65279725}},<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Emperor Tenmu, 9th year, July 25 entry</ref> and in January of the 1st year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|WD=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō|1=Empress Jitō}} ({{ill|687|en|687|WD=Q30303|lt=687|1=687}}) with {{ill|Abe Goshujin|ja|阿倍御主人|lt=Fuse Goshujin|lt_ja=布勢御主人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Goshujin|1=Abe no Miushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657245}}.<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Empress Jitō, January 1 entry</ref> Since no ministers were appointed during the Tenmu period, the position of Nagon was the highest administrative office. The title “Nagon” is of Chinese origin, as seen in the "Shuntian" of the {{ill|Book of Documents|en|Book of Documents|WD=Q875313|lt=Shu Jing|1=Book of Documents}}, and in the early Sui and Tang dynasties it was the title for the head ({{ill|Palace Attendant|en|Palace Attendant|WD=Q5368120|lt=Chamberlain|1=Palace Attendant}}) of the {{ill|Menxia Sheng|en|Menxia Sheng|WD=Q10839649|lt=Monkashō|1=Menxia Sheng}}.
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479774}} * Mother: Unknown * Children by an unknown consort: ** Son: {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose no Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11479785}} ** Son: {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kooharu|1=Kooharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q95162107}}

'''Kose no Kuromaro''' (巨勢 黒麻呂) is an aristocrat of the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka Period|1=Asuka period}}. His name is also recorded as '''Kuromaru'''. His {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|qid=Q1664099|lt=Surname|1=Kabane}} is of the type {{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|qid=Q2574718|lt=Subject|1=Omi (title)}}. He was the son of the {{ill|Minister of the Left|en|Minister of the Left|qid=Q949008|lt=Minister of the Left|1=Minister of the Left}} {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11479774}}, and the father of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose no Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11479785}} and {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kooharu|1=Kooharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q95162107}}.<ref>'Kōkei Honin': Clause of Kose no Asomi Sakaimaro in Tenpyō 9</ref> His court Rank, as indicated by the {{ill|26‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位二十六階|lt=Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/26‐step Court Rank|1=26-Rank cap system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17209277}}, was that of {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshikinchu|1=Shōkinchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11464366}} and {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|qid=Q1185382|lt=Chunagon|1=Chūnagon}}, though there are doubts regarding his official positions.
== Analysis == Although many details of his career and life remain unclear, from the obituary of {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose no Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11479785}} in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|qid=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}}<ref>'Shoku Nihongi': Kamiki 1, June 6 entry</ref> the following can be ascertained: # His court Rank, {{ill|Koshikinchu|ja|小錦中|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshikinchu|1=Shōkinchū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11464366}}, was the 11th Rank of the 26‐step system established in February of the 3rd year of {{ill|Emperor Tenji|en|Emperor Tenji|qid=Q314802|lt=Emperor Tenji|1=Emperor Tenji}}’s reign ({{ill|664|en|664|qid=Q30621|lt=664|1=664}}), and although it was a high Rank, by January of the 14th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}}’s reign ({{ill|685|en|685|qid=Q33480|lt=685|1=685}}) the {{ill|48‐step Court Rank|ja|冠位・位階制度の変遷#冠位四十八階|lt=48‐step Court Rank|lt_ja=冠位四十八階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/48‐step Court Rank|1=Transition of Crown and Court Ranks|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11395032}} was established. # {{ill|Chūnagon|en|Chūnagon|qid=Q1185382|lt=Chunagon|1=Chūnagon}} was established in April of the 2nd year of {{ill|Keiun|en|Keiun|qid=Q428753|lt=Keiun|1=Keiun}} ({{ill|705|en|705|qid=Q23504|lt=705|1=705}}) under the title of {{ill|Reigai-kan|ja|令外官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reigai-kan|1=East Asian extra-statutory office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11378315}}, a position that did not exist during the Tenji and Tenmu periods. It is presumed that once established during the Tenmu period the character “中” was later inserted into the title “納言”.<ref>{{ill|Kazuo Aoki|ja|青木和夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazuo Aoki|1=Kazuo Aoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11661670}}: "Reading {{ill|Taku Tanaka|ja|田中卓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taku Tanaka|1=Takashi Tanaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11575370}}’s ‘Chunagon (Part 1)’" in ''Shoku Nihongi Kenkyū Ichi – Hachi''</ref> The title “Nagon” appears in instances such as in the 9th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}} ({{ill|680|en|680|qid=Q23453|lt=680|1=680}}) with {{ill|Toneri-ō|ja|舎人王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toneri-ō|1=Toneri no Ōkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q65279725}},<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Emperor Tenmu, 9th year, July 25 entry</ref> and in January of the 1st year of {{ill|Empress Jitō|en|Empress Jitō|qid=Q232026|lt=Empress Jitō|1=Empress Jitō}} ({{ill|687|en|687|qid=Q30303|lt=687|1=687}}) with {{ill|Abe Goshujin|ja|阿倍御主人|lt=Fuse Goshujin|lt_ja=布勢御主人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe Goshujin|1=Abe no Miushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11657245}}.<ref>'Nihon Shoki': Empress Jitō, January 1 entry</ref> Since no ministers were appointed during the Tenmu period, the position of Nagon was the highest administrative office. The title “Nagon” is of Chinese origin, as seen in the "Shuntian" of the {{ill|Book of Documents|en|Book of Documents|qid=Q875313|lt=Shu Jing|1=Book of Documents}}, and in the early Sui and Tang dynasties it was the title for the head ({{ill|Palace Attendant|en|Palace Attendant|qid=Q5368120|lt=Chamberlain|1=Palace Attendant}}) of the {{ill|Menxia Sheng|en|Menxia Sheng|qid=Q10839649|lt=Monkashō|1=Menxia Sheng}}.
== Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Kose Tokuda|ja|巨勢徳多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Tokuda|1=Kose no Tokuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11479774}} * Mother: Unknown * Children by an unknown consort: ** Son: {{ill|Kose Oharu|ja|巨勢邑治|lt=Oharu|lt_ja=邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Oharu|1=Kose no Ōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11479785}} ** Son: {{ill|Kose Kooharu|ja|巨勢小邑治|lt=Kooharu|lt_ja=小邑治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose Kooharu|1=Kooharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q95162107}}
